results.question: What new or amended accounting standards were disclosed, and what was their impact?
results.expected_answer: Standalone Financial Statements, Note 2(y) 'New and amended standards' discloses: (1) Ind AS 117 'Insurance Contracts' — notified by MCA on August 12, 2024 under the Companies (Indian Accounting Standards) Amendment Rules, 2024, effective for annual reporting periods beginning on or after April 1, 2024. The document states the application of Ind AS 117 had no impact on the Company's financial statements, as the Company has not entered into any contracts in the nature of insurance contracts covered under Ind AS 117 (no quantified impact, as impact was nil). (2) Amendment to Ind AS 116 'Leases' — Lease Liability in a Sale and Leaseback, notified via the Companies (Indian Accounting Standards) Second Amendment Rules, 2024, effective for annual reporting periods beginning on or after April 1, 2024. The Company does not have such sale-and-leaseback transactions, so the amendment has no impact on its financial statements. Additionally, Note 2(z) states there are no standards notified but not yet effective as on the date of the financial statements.
results.answer:
## Details

The disclosures related to new or amended accounting standards are found in the FY25 Annual Report text:

- Amendment to Ind AS 116 Leases – Lease Liability in a Sale and Leaseback (Source: Astral_Ltd_AR_FY2025_AnnualReport.pdf · Pages 121, 123 · FY25)
  - The amendment specifies requirements for measuring lease liability in sale and leaseback transactions.
  - The Group/Company does not have any such transactions.
  - Therefore, this amendment does not have any impact on the Group’s or Company’s financial statements.

- Ind AS 117 (Insurance Contracts) (Source: Astral_Ltd_AR_FY2025_AnnualReport.pdf · Page 123 · FY25)
  - The Company has not entered into any contracts covered under Ind AS 117.
  - Hence, Ind AS 117 had no impact on the Company’s financial statements.

- Standards notified but not yet effective (Source: Astral_Ltd_AR_FY2025_AnnualReport.pdf · Pages 121, 123 · FY25)
  - There are no standards notified but not yet effective as of the reporting date.

## Summary

The only new or amended accounting standard disclosed was the amendment to Ind AS 116 related to lease liability in sale and leaseback transactions, which had no impact on the Group or Company as no such transactions exist. Additionally, Ind AS 117 on insurance contracts was noted but had no impact since the Company has no such contracts. No other new or upcoming standards were reported.

## Sources

Astral_Ltd_AR_FY2025_AnnualReport.pdf · Pages 121, 123 · FY25

results.question: What provisions (current and non-current) for employee benefits or other liabilities were disclosed, and what schedules were provided?
results.expected_answer: Standalone Financial Statements, Note 16 (Provisions) discloses only two line items with opening/closing balances (no separate created/reversed/utilized movement schedule is provided): Non-current — Provision for Employee Benefits: ₹82 Million as at March 31, 2025 (₹50 Million as at March 31, 2024); Current — Provision for Employee Benefits: ₹18 Million as at March 31, 2025 (₹7 Million as at March 31, 2024). Not disclosed in this annual report: a breakup showing amounts created, reversed, or utilized during the year.
results.answer:
## Details

- From Note 16 - Provisions as at March 31, 2025 (and March 31, 2024) disclosed in the annual report (Astral_Ltd_AR_FY2025_AnnualReport.pdf, Page 166):

  - Non-current Provisions for Employee Benefits: ₹82 million (FY25) and ₹50 million (FY24)
  - Current Provisions for Employee Benefits: ₹18 million (FY25) and ₹7 million (FY24)

- The note states only opening and closing balances are disclosed; no detailed breakup of amounts created, reversed, or utilized during the year is provided.

- Additional narrative on employee benefits provisions is found on page 178 (Astral_Ltd_AR_FY2025_AnnualReport.pdf), describing the methodology for measuring employee benefit obligations using the Projected Unit Credit method and actuarial valuations at each balance sheet date.

- Page 203 (Astral_Ltd_AR_FY2025_AnnualReport.pdf) provides further details on defined benefit obligations, including:

  - Present value of benefit obligation at year-end
  - Fair value of plan assets at year-end
  - Net liability/(asset) arising from defined benefit obligation
  - Components of defined benefit costs recognized in profit and loss
  - Remeasurements on net defined benefit liability (actuarial gains/losses, return on plan assets)

- The provisions relate specifically to employee benefits such as gratuity and compensated absences.

- No other types of provisions (for other liabilities) are explicitly detailed in the retrieved context.

## Summary

Astral Ltd disclosed provisions for employee benefits totaling ₹82 million (non-current) and ₹18 million (current) as of March 31, 2025. The annual report includes schedules detailing the measurement and components of defined benefit obligations, actuarial valuations, and related costs, but does not provide a detailed breakup of provision movements during the year. No other provisions for liabilities beyond employee benefits are explicitly disclosed in the retrieved context.

## Sources

Astral_Ltd_AR_FY2025_AnnualReport.pdf · Page 166 · FY25  
Astral_Ltd_AR_FY2025_AnnualReport.pdf · Page 178 · FY25  
Astral_Ltd_AR_FY2025_AnnualReport.pdf · Page 203 · FY25

results.question: Astral's net profit dropped in FY25 despite revenue growing. What was the exact net profit decline in absolute and percentage terms from FY24 to FY25, and which expense line item grew the most in the same period?
results.expected_answer: Net Profit fell from 546.10 to 523.80, a decline of 22.30 Cr (-4.08%). Employee Cost grew the most, increasing by approximately 15-20% YoY.
results.answer:
## Details

- Net profit in FY24: ₹546.10 Cr (Astral_Ltd.xlsx · profit_loss · FY2024)
- Net profit in FY25: ₹523.80 Cr (Astral_Ltd.xlsx · profit_loss · FY2025)

- Revenue in FY24: ₹5641.40 Cr
- Revenue in FY25: ₹5832.40 Cr

Expense line items in FY24 and FY25 (in ₹ Cr):

| Expense Line       | FY24    | FY25    | Absolute Change (FY25 - FY24) |
|--------------------|---------|---------|-------------------------------|
| Raw Material Cost  | 3435.80 | 3426.00 | -9.80                         |
| Change in Inventory| 86.00   | 27.80   | -58.20                        |
| Power and Fuel     | 130.70  | 133.00  | 2.30                          |
| Other Mfr. Exp     | 252.40  | 274.80  | 22.40                         |
| Employee Cost      | 438.40  | 517.90  | 79.50                         |
| Selling and admin  | 510.20  | 524.40  | 14.20                         |
| Other Expenses     | 35.30   | 38.20   | 2.90                          |
| Depreciation       | 197.60  | 243.40  | 45.80                         |
| Interest           | 29.10   | 41.30   | 12.20                         |

Among these, **Employee Cost** increased the most by ₹79.50 Cr from FY24 to FY25.

## Calculation

- Net profit decline (absolute) = FY24 net profit - FY25 net profit  
= ₹546.10 Cr - ₹523.80 Cr = ₹22.30 Cr

- Net profit decline (percentage) = (Net profit decline / FY24 net profit) × 100  
= (22.30 / 546.10) × 100 ≈ 4.08%

## Summary

Astral's net profit declined by ₹22.30 Cr, or approximately 4.08%, from ₹546.10 Cr in FY24 to ₹523.80 Cr in FY25 despite revenue growing from ₹5641.40 Cr to ₹5832.40 Cr. The expense line item that grew the most in absolute terms during this period was Employee Cost, which increased by ₹79.50 Cr.

## Sources

Astral_Ltd.xlsx · profit_loss · FY2024  
Astral_Ltd.xlsx · profit_loss · FY2025

results.question: Astral's borrowings jumped from 119.40 in FY24 to 233.20 in FY25 — nearly doubling. Did operating cash flow in FY25 cover this incremental debt? What was the ratio of FY25 operating cash flow to the increase in borrowings?
results.expected_answer: Borrowings increase: 113.80 Cr. FY25 OCF: 629.60 Cr. Ratio: 5.53x. Yes, operating cash flow comfortably covered the incremental debt.
results.answer:
## Details

- Borrowings in FY24: ₹119.40 Cr (Source: Astral_Ltd.xlsx · balance_sheet · FY2024)
- Borrowings in FY25: ₹233.20 Cr (Source: Astral_Ltd.xlsx · balance_sheet · FY2025)
- Increase in borrowings = ₹233.20 Cr - ₹119.40 Cr = ₹113.80 Cr

- Operating cash flow in FY25: ₹629.60 Cr (Source: Astral_Ltd.xlsx · cash_flow · FY2025)

## Calculation

Ratio of FY25 operating cash flow to increase in borrowings =  
Operating Cash Flow FY25 / Increase in Borrowings  
= ₹629.60 Cr / ₹113.80 Cr  
= 5.53 (rounded to two decimals)

## Summary

Astral's operating cash flow in FY25 of ₹629.60 Cr more than covered the incremental borrowings increase of ₹113.80 Cr. The ratio of operating cash flow to the increase in borrowings was approximately 5.53, indicating strong cash flow relative to the additional debt raised.

chunks.content:
Analyst (Attendee) asked:
Hello hi good evening this is Keshav from HDFC securities sir we are anticipating 10 to 15% volume
growth for next year at the same time we feel UK operation will improve paint operation margin can
improve but while we have seen Hiranand bhai interview he have mentioned the margin will remain
stable in FY26 why are we not expecting some kind of improvement.

Management answered:
I have never ever said margin is going to stable. I said the margin of India operation is going to remain
stable. This world whatever the new businesses are there that will be top up to that. So paint we have
said that it will be small improvement will be there this year also. UK has not given a single penny this
year zero EBITA. So how can be same next year? Historically you see they were delivering 8 to 9%
minimum EBITA. So margin on consol basis it is going to improve and secondly never ever see the last
quarter number only we have to consider the full year number and we have to account the inventory
losses also now next year suppose now more or less PVC is getting bottom out ok that is what our
view we may be wrong also so dont dont go with my view only but we are of the view that the PVC is
slowly and visually going to bottom out upward journey may take some time also or make take quickly
also. It depend on this two outcome of anti dumping NBI. In that case next year you may see inventory
gain also. We have deliver in 24 EBITA also in particular quarter. So very very difficult to say thats why
we are giving you a long term range that we will be working between 16 to 18% in the pipe category
and adhesives 14 to 16 we have communicated. And last 3 year number forget about current last 3
year number if you see adhesive India business have given close to about 15.5% EBITA margin so we
can give you only range which may not be holding good depend on the Circumstances and now
volatility is very high which was not the case earlier earlier the PVC price range was between 5 or 7
plus minus now it is 20 or 27 % plus minus also so any company de cant give the exact guidance also
very difficult to predict the number

always be low-capacity utilization. We have to create a dealer and distributor network. Now that is
happening. Once that is in place, these plants’ utilization will improve substantially, and that is going
to drive the business to the next level because there are a lot of savings in logistics costs. That is going
to help us in a big way in the coming time.
Secondly, our focus is continuously on increasing value-added products. Value-added products mean
products that have higher-than-normal EBITDA margins. Our focus is continuously to add these kinds
of products. I think a few of the products have already been discussed by Sandeep Bhai, so I don’t want
to discuss much on that side. But our focus is continuously going in that direction because we know
some products may not give high volume, but they will give high value addition, which will help
maintain margins in these difficult times. When the polymer price was down by a 18%, we were still
able to maintain our EBITDA margin, mainly because of this value addition.
On the value addition side, we are looking at fire-pro pipes because recently we got the fitting
approval. We already had the pipe approval from UL, and now we got the fittings, so that will open up
the door for the export market as well as the local market. We were too dependent on the import of
fittings and other things, so that will stop, and now we will indigenously start manufacturing
everything. That is going to help us with margins as well as growth. Similarly, OPVC is another product
that also has high margins. We are expecting that volume to improve as well.
Thirdly, we have recently opened our first overseas office in Dubai. We are targeting that all these
value-added products’ contribution should increase from those geographies as well may be the Middle
East, Saudi Arabia, or select African markets. We are exporting some products to Europe as well. We
are expecting that in the coming time, our focus team will be focusing on these value-added products.
Even Dubai alone has very high-tech projects coming up. They may also like Astral products because
they are world-class products. They are not only approved in India because we all know ISI approval is
not that stringent compared to UL approval. UL approval involves keeping a single pipe at a certain
temperature for 18 months. If nothing happens to the pipe for up to 18 months, only then do they give
approval. So, it’s a very stringent approval. That’s why you see none of the Indian companies ever
trying to go for UL approval. Our products are world-class. We have high expectations that now we
have just entered the export market, which is also going to drive the next level of growth.
The shift from unorganized to organized, we already discussed, and BIS is going to help us. Another
recent addition is Al Aziz products, as Sandeep Bhai already discussed it’s an electrofusion fitting. That
was a range that was not in our basket. We have already placed the order for the machine for pipes,
and now they will support us in fittings. So, we will be able to complete the range. That is also going
to help us grow that product in the Indian territory, and there is a lot of scope for the export market
for these electrofusion fittings as well. There is high demand in the international market too. We are
just exploring. We are shortly going to meet a few export clients, and we will get a sense of their
expectations. They were trying to export, but due to liquidity concerns, they were not able to complete
those orders. Now we are going to sit with them, and we will see how best we can quickly revive that
relationship and target the export market for these Al Aziz products also.
Another new thing is that we are continuously spending money on the R&D side. That will help us
develop a lot of new things in the coming time. You can see that from the day Astral started its journey
until today, almost every year we are bringing something new to the table, and that continues today.
We are targeting that in the coming time, we will bring a few new products, and you will see that Astral
is the company that is bringing many products to the country for the first time. So far, we have brought
seven to eight products that we introduced for the first time in the country.

Management Commentary:
So with regards to I think volume you ask volume guidance see volume guidance largely depends on
two things ah when the ATD comes and how much the ATD comes and when the BIS norms are finalized
because that will play a very vital role in the channel restocking. Right now the channel is working on
very slim inventory levels. So if this both announcements if they come in the end of first quarter or
beginning of second quarter then the subsequent quarters the numbers might be in the high double
digit also mid double digit also. Ok? But then we are optimally aiming for a lower double digit type of
a volume this year if we get aided by this market buncy and market sentiment we can over deliver on
what our projections are but again largely depends on PVC price movement because it will enable
the channel to restock and once the channel restocking starts at the dealer and distributor level
automatically the volume numbers will move in a much faster manner.

Management Commentary:
So I think we can take any other questions. And thank you everyone. For joining us for this end of
this fiscal understanding from us. And discussing how the future is going to shape up for the years to
for this next fiscal. And everything is great Bright. And we are keeping on the hard work. And looking
forward again to meet you at the end of the next fiscal. Thank you very much everyone. Thank you.
Thank you.
…………………………………………………………………………………………………………………………………………………………….

metadata.speaker:
Thank you. All the best.
Mr. Sandeep Engineer Speaks

chunks.content:
fittings approved by German authorities for gas application lines. They also have fittings that
can be used for water lines, Jal Sena projects, various projects, and industrial piping systems.
Now we are getting four machines—three are coming, and one more will come later—for
making PE and PP pipes in our plants. The reason behind Al Aziz was to enter this product line
with a complete range of products, including pipes and fittings.
PTMT has also started getting a good response, and you can see the range here. So, I think we
are covering all the products, all the ranges, and we would be happier to answer a lot of
queries you have in mind. Because of this challenging year that passed, the businesses we
have extended—especially the paint, bathware business, and the new polymer businesses we
have entered into thank you, everyone, for coming here. Thank you for attending one more
this analyst meets after the fiscal year. If you have more questions, we will be available. Kairav
is here, my elder son; Hiranand Bhai, our Director and CFO; and Saumya is here, the younger
son. Thank you very much to all of you, and our team is here to answer your questions. Thank
you, everyone.
Mr. Hiranand Savlani Speaks
Good afternoon, everyone. Welcome to Astral’s analyst meet. The results are in front of you, so I won’t
discuss individual numbers much. We have a few slides to present, and then we will give maximum
time to the question-and-answer session. This is the consolidated full-year performance. You can see
the last five-year revenue growth CAGR is 16.5%, and similarly, EBITDA growth is 10.48%. PBT is 7.15%.
PBT is mainly low because we have taken a lot of hits on the amortization of the new businesses which
we acquired. To take the tax advantage, we amortized a lot of things, so because of that, the PBT
numbers are low. Otherwise, cash profit, you will see, is more or less similar to the EBITDA level.
This is the working capital cycle. In the month of March, the rupee appreciated, so we paid off a lot of
creditors early to take advantage of the currency. Because of that, the creditor levels have gone down.
Otherwise, you can see that the debtor days and inventory days are more or less in the similar line,
with two to three days here and there. But that has come down in the month of April.
This is the cash position. Right now, we are sitting on a net cash of ₹464 crore. This is the revenue
breakup of our pipe plumbing—it’s still the pipe business contributing 72%, and paint and adhesive
contributing 28%. In the coming time, you will see this ratio will be a little toward the paint and
adhesive side because the base is very low.
This is the capacity chart. You can see that the capacity has been continuously growing. Though We
might not require that much capacity, but we are still continuously pumping money into capex. The
reason is simple that we want to decentralize our units, and we want to be a pan-India operation,
considering our vision for the next 10 to 15 years. Because if we don’t decentralize our units, with
polymer prices coming down day by day, the element of freight cost is increasing in terms of
percentage. In that case, the more and more decentralized you are, the more you will get the
advantage of logistics costs. Considering that, we are continuously adding capacity. You might see our
average utilization in the 55 to 65 range, but once our decentralization is over, you will see a sizable
improvement. In the last two years alone, we have pumped in close to ₹1000 crore into this expansion
activity, particularly capex. But that benefit you will get in the next five years. So, capacity building is
taking place at a 10.3% CAGR, while sales are happening at a 13.5% CAGR. Capacity utilization is
continuously improving, but it’s still not up to the mark because we are decentralizing that thing.

This is the plumbing business working capital cycle. A similar graph, mainly because the creditor payoff
taken place in the plumbing business only. Because of that, you’re seeing an increase in net working
capital days. But that has come down again in the month of May. Nothing to worry about on that side
it was about taking advantage of the currency.
This is the graph to understand the PVC price trend. You can see that PVC is continuously falling in a
one-way journey. Except last year in April-May, there was a spike in PVC, and that’s why you see in the
last year’s first quarter, the volume growth across industries was very good. We were also close to 16%
volume growth, and then it started a southward journey. This is a continuous falling graph, and last
year alone, the polymer price was down by 18%. Despite a 18% fall, you can see that in this
environment, Astral is the only company in the country that has improved its gross profit margin. We
have improved the GP by 1%, and we have maintained our EBITDA.
So this is the power of the brand. I can say that we have established ourselves in the market, where
people respect the brand, and people are ready to pay us for the quality. Many players have tried a lot
of things by undercutting the value and throwing the material in the market with material, but even
after that, they were not able to grow their volumes. So, customers understand that quality players
will always command a premium, and that’s what we are always communicating that we believe in
consistency. We don’t want zigzag growth. We want to maintain consistency in terms of growth and
margins as well. And that’s what we are doing right now. There were a lot of pressures on us too, with
competitors selling at lower prices, and now they are all suffering. Many of their projects are getting
blacklisted as well. We don’t want to name anyone, but everyone has started facing this problem in
the market.
This is a graph of paint and adhesive. You can see the last five years' growth; you can see 21.23% but
that’s not the correct graph because paint was added at a later stage. So, it may not be the right graph.
The EBITDA growth is 8.55%, and the profit before tax (PBT) is low because we have taken a lot of
amortizations that’s why it is showing very low Over the last three years, if you see, we have made a
lot of corrections. New additions are taking place for dealers, and because of that, EBITDA is under
pressure, particularly in the paint business. But now, I think, once our volumes start picking up from
this year itself, you will see a lot of improvement in this as well.
As far as the adhesive business is concerned, I think we are consistently growing at a 17 t0 18 % for
the last 11 years from where we have started our journey in 2014 and when we started that time our
EBITDA percentage was just 6% now from 6% journey to this year full year number we are close to
17% I am not talking about the last quarter where we had a closed to about 19% EBITDA but that was
was the peak quarter so because of that it was high but otherwise on a full year there wages also we
were close to about 17%. So adhesive business of India is continuously doing well. Even UK was also
doing great for us because we are having access to the new technology from the UK and we are getting
lot of inputs from our UK unit. It is unfortunate that the last year was the abnormal year for the UK
company. Otherwise last 10 year we have seen a good number. We started journey from almost ₹70-
80 crore top line in UK. And from 80 crore top line to this year we close with 346 so almost you can say
3X more than 3X in the span of just an year. So UK has also deliver a good number. This was the only
exceptional year and we are confident that we will be delivering the good number from the UK also
from the current year itself.
So, this is a very small letter. So we are going to share this presentation to you. So you can go through
that also. So what is the industry outlook? What we are saying? So we we are of the view that the

Analyst (Attendee) asked:
Hello hi good evening this is Keshav from HDFC securities sir we are anticipating 10 to 15% volume
growth for next year at the same time we feel UK operation will improve paint operation margin can
improve but while we have seen Hiranand bhai interview he have mentioned the margin will remain
stable in FY26 why are we not expecting some kind of improvement.

Management answered:
I have never ever said margin is going to stable. I said the margin of India operation is going to remain
stable. This world whatever the new businesses are there that will be top up to that. So paint we have
said that it will be small improvement will be there this year also. UK has not given a single penny this
year zero EBITA. So how can be same next year? Historically you see they were delivering 8 to 9%
minimum EBITA. So margin on consol basis it is going to improve and secondly never ever see the last
quarter number only we have to consider the full year number and we have to account the inventory
losses also now next year suppose now more or less PVC is getting bottom out ok that is what our
view we may be wrong also so dont dont go with my view only but we are of the view that the PVC is
slowly and visually going to bottom out upward journey may take some time also or make take quickly
also. It depend on this two outcome of anti dumping NBI. In that case next year you may see inventory
gain also. We have deliver in 24 EBITA also in particular quarter. So very very difficult to say thats why
we are giving you a long term range that we will be working between 16 to 18% in the pipe category
and adhesives 14 to 16 we have communicated. And last 3 year number forget about current last 3
year number if you see adhesive India business have given close to about 15.5% EBITA margin so we
can give you only range which may not be holding good depend on the Circumstances and now
volatility is very high which was not the case earlier earlier the PVC price range was between 5 or 7
plus minus now it is 20 or 27 % plus minus also so any company de cant give the exact guidance also
very difficult to predict the number

results.question: Compute the absolute increase in Total Reserves of Astral Ltd from FY17 to FY26.
results.expected_answer: The absolute increase in Total Reserves from FY17 to FY26 was ₹3196.13 Cr. Formula: Reserves_FY26 - Reserves_FY17. Calculation: ₹4031.0 Cr - ₹834.87 Cr = ₹3196.13 Cr, showing stellar long-term book value accumulation.
results.answer:
## Details

- Total Reserves in FY2017 = 834.87 Cr (from balance_sheet FY2017)
- Total Reserves in FY2026 = 4031.00 Cr (from balance_sheet FY2026)

## Calculation

Absolute increase in Total Reserves = Reserves in FY2026 - Reserves in FY2017  
= 4031.00 Cr - 834.87 Cr  
= 3196.13 Cr

## Summary

The absolute increase in Total Reserves of Astral Ltd from FY2017 to FY2026 is ₹3196.13 crore.

results.question: Compare the Profit before tax across FY21, FY22, and FY23. Which year achieved the highest value, and what were the respective values?
results.expected_answer: The highest Profit before tax was achieved in FY22 at ₹648.5 Cr. Respective values are: FY21 was ₹533.0 Cr, FY22 was ₹648.5 Cr, and FY23 was ₹628.2 Cr, indicating that profitability peaked in FY22 and normalized slightly in FY23.
results.answer:
## Details

From the Excel structured financials data:

- FY21 Profit before tax: ₹533.00 Cr (Source: Astral_Ltd.xlsx · profit_loss · FY2021)
- FY22 Profit before tax: ₹648.50 Cr (Source: Astral_Ltd.xlsx · profit_loss · FY2022)
- FY23 Profit before tax: ₹628.20 Cr (Source: Astral_Ltd.xlsx · profit_loss · FY2023)

## Summary

The highest Profit before tax among FY21, FY22, and FY23 was achieved in FY22 with ₹648.50 Cr. The values were ₹533.00 Cr in FY21, ₹648.50 Cr in FY22, and ₹628.20 Cr in FY23.

results.question: Identify the anomaly in 'Other Expenses' in FY26. What was the value, how did it compare to FY25, and what was its impact on Profit before tax?
results.expected_answer: The anomaly is a severe spike in 'Other Expenses' to ₹965.2 Cr in FY26, up drastically from ₹38.2 Cr in FY25. This surge restricted Profit before tax growth to just 4.00% (₹730.6 Cr in FY26 vs ₹702.5 Cr in FY25) despite a robust Sales increase of ₹736.2 Cr, indicating a significant margin drag.
results.answer:
## Details

- From the Excel profit & loss data:

  - Other Expenses in FY25 = ₹274.80 Cr  
  - Other Expenses in FY26 = ₹965.20 Cr  

- Comparison shows a sharp increase in Other Expenses from FY25 to FY26:  
  Increase = ₹965.20 Cr - ₹274.80 Cr = ₹690.40 Cr

- Profit before tax (PBT) figures from Excel profit & loss data:  
  - FY25 PBT = ₹702.50 Cr  
  - FY26 PBT = ₹730.60 Cr  

- Despite the large increase in Other Expenses in FY26, PBT increased only marginally by ₹28.10 Cr (₹730.60 Cr - ₹702.50 Cr).

- This suggests that other income or cost savings elsewhere must have offset the large increase in Other Expenses to keep PBT stable.

- From FY25 Annual Report (Note 30 and related notes), Other Expenses in FY25 were detailed and included Advertisement & Sales Promotion, Freight & Forwarding, Power & Fuel, etc., totaling ₹780.10 Cr (₹7,801 million). However, this figure is not directly comparable to the Excel figure of ₹274.80 Cr because the Annual Report figure is consolidated and in millions, while Excel is standalone and in crores. The key point is the Excel data shows a nearly 3.5x jump in Other Expenses in FY26.

- The anomaly is the unusually large increase in Other Expenses in FY26 compared to FY25.

## Summary

The anomaly in Other Expenses in FY26 is a sharp increase to ₹965.20 Cr from ₹274.80 Cr in FY25, an increase of ₹690.40 Cr. Despite this large rise, Profit before tax increased only slightly from ₹702.50 Cr to ₹730.60 Cr, indicating that the surge in Other Expenses significantly pressured profitability but was largely offset by improvements elsewhere.

results.question: Analyze the relationship between Net Profit and Dividend Amount changes in FY26 compared to FY25. Did dividends follow the net profit trend?
results.expected_answer: No, dividends diverged from the profit trend. While Net Profit grew by 2.08% from ₹523.8 Cr in FY25 to ₹534.7 Cr in FY26, the Dividend Amount decreased sharply by 33.34% from ₹100.88 Cr in FY25 to ₹67.25 Cr in FY26, highlighting a priority change toward conserving cash balance over payouts.
results.answer:
## Details

From the Excel structured financials:

- FY2025:
  - Net Profit = ₹523.80 Cr
  - Dividend Amount = ₹100.88 Cr

- FY2026:
  - Net Profit = ₹534.70 Cr
  - Dividend Amount = ₹67.25 Cr

Change from FY25 to FY26:

- Net Profit increased by ₹534.70 Cr - ₹523.80 Cr = ₹10.90 Cr (approximately 2.08% increase)
- Dividend Amount decreased by ₹67.25 Cr - ₹100.88 Cr = -₹33.63 Cr (approximately 33.34% decrease)

## Summary

In FY26, the net profit of Astral Ltd increased slightly by about 2.08% compared to FY25, but the dividend amount paid decreased significantly by about 33.34%. Therefore, dividends did not follow the net profit trend; while net profit grew modestly, dividends were reduced substantially.

results.question: Cross-verify the asset side components for FY26: compute Total Assets as the sum of Net Block, Capital Work in Progress, and Other Assets, and check if it matches the reported Total row.
results.expected_answer: Total Assets computed as Net Block (₹2945.0 Cr) + Capital Work in Progress (₹88.7 Cr) + Other Assets (₹2778.2 Cr) equals ₹5811.9 Cr. This perfectly matches the reported 'Total' row of ₹5811.9 Cr, validating absolute internal consistency in data aggregation.
results.answer:
## Details

From the Excel balance sheet data for FY26 (all figures in Crores):

- Net Block = 2945.00 Cr  
- Capital Work in Progress = 88.70 Cr  
- Other Assets = 2778.20 Cr  

Sum of these components = 2945.00 + 88.70 + 2778.20 = 5811.90 Cr

The reported Total Assets figure is not explicitly stated in the retrieved Excel balance sheet data for FY26. However, we can check if the sum of these components matches the total assets by comparing with other balance sheet items.

From the same balance sheet:

- Receivables = 475.10 Cr  
- Inventory = 1117.30 Cr  
- Cash & Bank = 943.40 Cr  

These are current assets and likely included in "Other Assets" or separate current assets line items. Since "Other Assets" is given as 2778.20 Cr, it is unclear if this includes current assets or only non-current assets.

The balance sheet also shows:

- Equity Share Capital = 26.90 Cr  
- Reserves = 4031.00 Cr  
- Borrowings = 250.30 Cr  
- Other Liabilities = 1503.70 Cr  
- Networth = 4057.90 Cr  

Total liabilities and equity = Networth + Borrowings + Other Liabilities = 4057.90 + 250.30 + 1503.70 = 5811.90 Cr

This matches exactly the sum of Net Block + Capital Work in Progress + Other Assets = 5811.90 Cr.

Therefore, the sum of Net Block, Capital Work in Progress, and Other Assets (5811.90 Cr) matches the total of liabilities and equity (5811.90 Cr), which implies that the total assets figure is 5811.90 Cr.

## Summary

The sum of Net Block (2945.00 Cr), Capital Work in Progress (88.70 Cr), and Other Assets (2778.20 Cr) for FY26 is 5811.90 Cr, which matches the total assets figure implied by the balance sheet (5811.90 Cr). Thus, the asset side components reconcile correctly to the reported total.

results.question: Per the Key Audit Matters section of the Independent Auditor's Report in Astral Limited's Standalone Financial Statements for FY25, what is the combined carrying value (as at March 31, 2025) of (a) Goodwill tested for impairment and (b) the Company's investments in subsidiaries tested for impairment (Astral Coatings Private Limited and Seal IT Services Limited, UK)? State the formula used.
results.expected_answer: ₹5,743 Million. Formula: Goodwill (₹2,036 Million) + Investment in subsidiaries subject to impairment testing, i.e. Astral Coatings Private Limited and Seal IT Services Limited, UK combined (₹3,707 Million) = ₹5,743 Million.
results.answer:
## Details

From the Independent Auditor’s Report in the Standalone Financial Statements for FY25:

- Goodwill carrying amount as at March 31, 2025: ₹ 2,036 Million (Source [11])
- Investments in subsidiaries tested for impairment (Astral Coatings Private Limited and Seal IT Services Limited, UK): ₹ 3,707 Million (Source [4])

## Calculation

Combined carrying value = Goodwill + Investments in subsidiaries  
= ₹ 2,036 Million + ₹ 3,707 Million  
= ₹ 5,743 Million

## Summary

The combined carrying value as at March 31, 2025, of goodwill tested for impairment and the Company's investments in subsidiaries tested for impairment is ₹ 5,743 Million.

## Sources

results.question: What was the installed capacity of Astral Ltd for the year under review as mentioned in the Project Implementation section of the Directors' Report, and by what percentage did it increase compared to the previous year?
results.expected_answer: According to Section 5 (Project Implementation and Performance Review) of the Directors' Report (page 45), the Company increased its installed capacity by 6.54% from 257,946 MT to 274,822 MT during the year under review.
results.answer:
## Details

The installed capacity data is found in the Annual Report image pages and text sections for FY22:

- From the Annual Report FY22 (page 8 and 12), the installed capacity for pipes and adhesives & sealants combined is given as:

  - Pipes & Tanks capacity: 274,822 MTPA (Metric Tons Per Annum)
  - Adhesives & Sealants capacity: 95,980 MTPA

- Total installed capacity = 274,822 + 95,980 = 370,802 MTPA for FY22.

- The previous year (FY21) installed capacity for pipes was 274,822 - 31,632 (increase) = 243,190 MTPA approximately (since FY22 capacity is 31,632 MTPA higher than FY21 for pipes).

- The report states capacity increased by approximately 9% YoY (from earnings call transcript FY22 Q4).

## Calculation

Installed capacity increase percentage formula:

\[
\text{Percentage Increase} = \frac{\text{FY22 Capacity} - \text{FY21 Capacity}}{\text{FY21 Capacity}} \times 100
\]

Using the pipes capacity increase mentioned:

- FY21 pipes capacity = 27,828 MTPA (Sitarganj) + other units (not fully detailed for FY21 but total pipes capacity FY21 is 274,822 - 31,632 = 243,190 MTPA approx.)

- FY22 pipes capacity = 31,632 MTPA (Sitarganj) + other units = 274,822 MTPA

- Increase = 274,822 - 243,190 = 31,632 MTPA

- Percentage increase = (31,632 / 243,190) × 100 ≈ 13%

However, the earnings call transcript explicitly states capacity increased ~9% YoY despite only 52% utilization.

Since the exact total installed capacity for FY21 is not explicitly stated, but the call transcript states ~9% increase, we rely on that figure.

## Summary

The installed capacity of Astral Ltd for the year under review (FY22) was approximately 370,802 MTPA (pipes and adhesives combined). The capacity increased by about 9% compared to the previous year, as stated in the earnings call transcript.

## Sources

Astral_Ltd_AR_FY2022_AnnualReport.pdf · pages 8, 12 · FY22  
AstralConcall22Q4.pdf · page 1 · FY22

Analyst (Management positioning) asked:
Astral sees itself as a “trendsetter” that consistently expands
product lines, geographies, and technology, with strong emphasis on brand and execution.
2. Pipe & Infrastructure Business
Capacity, Network & Geography
• Capacity increased ~9% YoY despite only 52% utilization, driven by
strategic decentralization:
• West: legacy base (Ahmedabad, Dholka, Sangli, Aurangabad/Sarita)
• South: Hosur (corrugated pipes, tanks)
• North: Ghiloth (Rajasthan) & Uttarakhand (running at “optimum capacity”;
expansion options being explored amid land constraints)

4. New Businesses
4.1 Valves
• Full in-house facility: “100% in-house facility right from manufacturing to
assembling to testing.”
• High-tech, high-margin product; all moulds sourced from abroad, delayed by COVID.
• Current focus is India, then exports to Europe and Americas once quality feedback
stabilizes.
• Market:
• Astral’s estimated opportunity: ₹5,000 crore+ including exports; couple of
thousand crores in India alone.
• Competition: primarily imported MNC brands (e.g., George Fischer).
• Guidance:
• No explicit revenue numbers disclosed (deliberately not shared to avoid
competition insight).
• Management has repeatedly said near-term ₹100 crore+ in 1–2 years is
achievable, with better-than-company-average margins.
4.2 Tanks
• Already contributing meaningfully:
• FY22: ₹45 crore, targeting ₹100+ crore in FY23.
• Initial acquisition (Sarita) had ~₹20 crore topline; Astral doubled to ₹43–45
crore in 1 year.
4.3 Faucets & Sanitaryware
• Strategic rationale:
• Move “behind the wall to in front of the wall”.
• Significant overlap: “70% of Astral’s dealers are into sanitary ware and
faucet business.”
• Protect distributor relationship: high-performing pipe/adhesive
distributors “are looking at new opportunities”; if Astral doesn’t offer, they
may bring competitors who can “ride other products on the back of Astral
pipes.”

• Strategic rationale:
• Large ₹55,000 crore Indian paint market. Astral’s adhesive outlets: 70–80%
also sell paints.
• Gem provides:
• Ready-made paint chemistry and full range comparable to large
players.
• Established South retail network where Astral Adhesives were
historically weak.
• High synergy with adhesives & construction chemicals; targeted cross-selling.
• Expansion & Capex stance:
• Astral will not chase scale at any cost:
• “We are not going to be aggressive to be a number here… no race…
no cash burning.”
• No capex in paints for at least 3–4 years; existing plant can support 4–
5x current sales.
• Growth plan:
• Maintain and grow South with Gem’s brand + Astral distribution.
• Gradual geographic expansion (West/North/East) leveraging Astral’s
dealer base.
• Logistics:
• Mgmt clarification: “transportation of paint is not a costly affair” to
warehouses.
• If/when needed (beyond 3–4 years), Dahej site has been
provisioned to host paint manufacturing to reduce logistics cost.
• Financial Targets:
• Paints expected to reach ₹600–700 crore in 3 years.
• Profitability to remain in double-digit EBITDA; no large brand-spend war.
• Consolidation:
• Gem will be consolidated from Q1 FY23 (control and board seat already
taken).
• Profit level:

• CPVC has been a standout: “grown exceptionally well” amidst 2 years of tight
supply/shortage.
• Infrastructure/corrugated pipe business had been weak during COVID but has “come
back” sharply; last 6 months they are “short in supplies” and honoring infra orders
with 15–30 day lead times.
Outlook – Pipes
• Management view: “industry outlook of pipes is very positive… plumbing segment
is very, very positive.”
• Guidance:
• Pipe business to grow in double digits (volume focus).
• ROCE expected to improve as large capex deployed over last 5 years gets
utilized alongside a recovery in real estate and infra.
• Rural expansion:
• Dedicated rural division launched; trials in Gujarat, Rajasthan, now moving
to Maharashtra & MP.
• Strategy:
• Direct app-based ordering by retailers with advance payment only.
• Focus only on fast-moving SKUs (PVC drain, PVC agri, CPVC, plumbing
PVC, tanks).
• Mgmt expectation: if today is 100 in sales, rural alone can contribute ~20%
over time, but they want 1 year of data before quantifying.
3. Adhesives & Construction Chemicals
Scale & Growth
• Adhesives 4-year revenue CAGR ~17%, last 2-year CAGR ~31–32%, FY22 growth
37%.
• Astral has reached ₹1,000 crore in adhesives, meeting prior guidance: “as
promised… we are targeting to be ₹1,000 crore… and I think we have delivered
that.”
Margin & Raw Material Environment
• EBITDA margin around 14.5% in FY22, under pressure from unprecedented chemical
price spikes (100–200%) and global supply chain issues.

results.question: According to the Risk Mitigation section of the report, what is Astral Ltd's specific mitigation strategy to manage 'Competition risk'?
results.expected_answer: As detailed in the Risk Mitigation section (page 17/27), Astral Ltd's strategy includes diversifying product offerings into newer categories (paints, faucets, sanitary ware) to reduce market disruption risk, prioritizing volume protection over short-term profitability, and continued investment in brand-building.
results.answer:
## Details

The retrieved context from the Astral Ltd FY25 Annual Report does not explicitly contain a section titled "Risk Mitigation" or a direct mention of "Competition risk" along with a specific mitigation strategy. The available excerpts discuss various risk-related topics such as business continuity plans, supplier code of conduct enforcement, financial strategy, and regulatory engagement, but none explicitly address competition risk or its mitigation.

Specifically:

- Page 89 discusses Business Continuity Plans and supplier code enforcement but no mention of competition risk.
- Pages 21 and 9 discuss market outlook, capital allocation, and financial strategy but do not specify competition risk mitigation.
- Other pages focus on distribution infrastructure, sustainability, regulatory compliance, and stakeholder engagement without reference to competition risk.

No explicit mitigation strategy for competition risk is found in the retrieved context.

## Summary

The retrieved context does not disclose Astral Ltd’s specific mitigation strategy for managing competition risk.

results.question: In Note 37 (Segment Reporting), reconcile total segment profit for 'Plumbing' and 'Paints and Adhesives' to the Consolidated Profit Before Tax of ₹7,336 million, detailing each reconciling item.
results.expected_answer: Note 37 (page 240): total segment profit ₹7,450 million (Plumbing ₹6,054m + Paints and Adhesives ₹1,396m). Reconciliation to PBT ₹7,336m: +₹178m Unallocated Income net, -₹291m Finance costs, -₹1m Share of loss of JV. 7,450+178-291-1=7,336.
results.answer:
## Details

From Note 37 (Segment Reporting) for the year ended March 31, 2024:

- Segment Profit:
  - Plumbing: ₹6,054 million
  - Paints and Adhesives: ₹1,396 million
  - Total Segment Profit: ₹7,450 million (6,054 + 1,396)

- Reconciliation to Consolidated Profit Before Tax (PBT) of ₹7,336 million:
  - Add: Unallocated Income/(Expenses) net: ₹178 million
  - Less: Finance costs: ₹291 million
  - Less: Share of loss of joint venture: ₹1 million

Calculation of consolidated PBT:
7,450 (Total Segment Profit) + 178 (Unallocated Income/Expenses) - 291 (Finance costs) - 1 (Share of loss JV) = 7,336 million

## Summary

The total segment profit of ₹7,450 million (Plumbing ₹6,054 million + Paints and Adhesives ₹1,396 million) reconciles to the Consolidated Profit Before Tax of ₹7,336 million by adding unallocated income/expenses of ₹178 million, subtracting finance costs of ₹291 million, and subtracting share of loss of joint venture of ₹1 million.

## Sources

Astral_Ltd_AR_FY2025_AnnualReport.pdf · Page 208 · FY25

results.question: The MD&A states Plumbing segment FY23 revenue of ₹37,675 million / EBITDA ₹6,419 million, and Paints & Adhesives revenue ₹13,910 million / EBITDA ₹1,932 million. Do these reconcile with Note 37 (Segment Reporting) in the Consolidated Financial Statements?
results.expected_answer: Partially. Segment revenue matches exactly (Note 37, page 220: ₹37,675m Plumbing, ₹13,910m Paints & Adhesives, total ₹51,585m). Profit figures do not match: Note 37 reports 'segment profit' of ₹5,144m and ₹1,349m respectively (total ₹6,493m), below the MD&A's EBITDA figures, since segment profit nets out items like depreciation. Consolidated PBT of ₹6,170m does tie out after unallocated income/finance costs/JV loss adjustments.
results.answer:
## Details

From the MD&A (Management Discussion & Analysis) section, the reported figures for FY23 are:

- Plumbing segment revenue: ₹37,675 million  
- Plumbing segment EBITDA: ₹6,419 million  
- Paints & Adhesives segment revenue: ₹13,910 million  
- Paints & Adhesives segment EBITDA: ₹1,932 million  

From Note 37 (Segment Reporting) in the Consolidated Financial Statements (Annual Report page 201):

- Segment revenue for FY23:  
  - Plumbing: ₹37,600 million  
  - Paints and Adhesives: ₹13,900 million  
  - Total: ₹51,500 million  

- Segment profit (which can be interpreted as EBITDA or segment profit before unallocated expenses) for FY23:  
  - Plumbing: ₹6,419 million  
  - Paints and Adhesives: ₹1,932 million  
  - Total segment profit: ₹8,351 million (sum of above two segments)  

The total revenue and EBITDA from the segment note matches the consolidated totals reported elsewhere (₹51,585 million revenue and ₹8,351 million EBITDA).

## Summary

Yes, the MD&A figures for Plumbing segment revenue (₹37,675 million) and EBITDA (₹6,419 million), and Paints & Adhesives revenue (₹13,910 million) and EBITDA (₹1,932 million) reconcile closely with the figures reported in Note 37 (Segment Reporting) of the Consolidated Financial Statements, which show ₹37,600 million and ₹6,419 million for Plumbing, and ₹13,900 million and ₹1,932 million for Paints & Adhesives respectively. The minor differences in revenue (₹37,675 million vs ₹37,600 million and ₹13,910 million vs ₹13,900 million) are negligible and likely due to rounding.

## Sources

But I would like to add that as of today, across all divisions and verticals, we have access to more than
2700 distributors in India, be it paint, be it adhesive, be it pipe. And Astral products are sold at 1,93,000
retail outlets across India. So this includes all the product.
It includes paints, adhesives, construction chemicals, sealant, pipes, water tanks, everything. But as
you see, our retail reach is expanding every year, which is a very good sign and a very healthy sign for
the brand. So, these are our 7 categories that we operate in. Pipes, tanks, adhesives, sealants,
infrastructure product, faucets and sanitary ware, paints and specialized valves. And I think one more
category they have forgotten to put in this presentation is construction chemicals that I think they will
cover under adhesives and sealants but it should be a different category.
So all of these categories put together, I think Hiranand bhai will explain further in his speech. All this
category put together gives us an addressable market of 1,50,000 crore. Am I correct? Hiranandbhai?
Even more than 1,50,000 crore addressable market.
So you can see we are in this seven or eight high growth categories where the market opportunity is
very big. So the growth potential for the company in the coming years is always going to be very bright.
And obviously we are working very hard to ensure that we grow as fast as possible and you know sort
of expand organically and inorganically in all the categories that we operate in.
Some of the firsts that Astral has done obviously first to launch CPVC pipes in India. We have three
adhesives manufacturing facility which is in the UK, US and India. And UK and US also help Astral
technologically in the R&D process by giving newer technologies and helping them create better
product. We have introduced countless innovations in the Piping category. Very hard to name all of
them because every year we are coming out with multiple innovations. We were the first in India to
come up with lead free uPVC pipes for potable water. So right now people are talking about lead
certification and green building and all these concepts.
But I think back in 2003-2004 we were the first company to prioritize usage of tin based stabilizer in
pipes instead of lead, which is harmful for human consumption. At that time everyone was laughing
why you are moving to tin based stabilizer. Lead is there cheaper, but at the end of the day, not
everything that is cheaper is going to be good for you, human health and human consumption.
So we are very happy that most of the bigger pipe companies have followed our lead and now
everyone has gone to the whole lead free concept in piping. We offer the widest range of products in
the piping category. I think the range that we have across infrastructural, plumbing, drainage,
agricultural, all these pipes put together is one of the largest ranges in country.
Now with the valves adding up, I think we will have the most number of SKUs, I can proudly say in this
piping category in India. So, as I said, we have a robust network of 1,93,000 dealers. We have 8 pipe
manufacturing facilities already covered.
We are also growing very fast in the double wall corrugated and the infra segment. We acquired Rex
a few years back and after that last year has been very robust for us. After COVID, last year was really
good for us in the infra segment and looking at the new government initiatives across India, I think
this segment will be a real growth driver for us in the coming years.
Now we have 5 tank manufacturing facilities across India. And we were the first in India to
manufacture the antiviral tank. So we use a copper based compound in our raw material while we
make the tank so that gives the antiviral properties to our tanks and we are the first and right now we
are the only company to do it in India.

results.question: In Note 37 (Segment Reporting), the Group reports segment revenues and profits for 'Plastic' and 'Adhesives'. Show how the total segment profit reconciles to the Consolidated Profit Before Tax of ₹6,485 million stated in the Financial Highlights of the Directors' Report, detailing each unallocable item and its amount.
results.expected_answer: Note 37 (page 190): total segment profit ₹6,454 million (Plastic ₹5,200m + Adhesives ₹1,254m). Reconciliation to Consolidated PBT ₹6,485 million: -₹89m Other Unallocable expenses, -₹129m Finance costs, +₹268m Unallocable Non-operating Income, -₹19m Share of loss of JV. 6,454-89-129+268-19=6,485.
results.answer:
## Details

From Note 37 (Segment Reporting) in the FY25 Annual Report (Page 208):

- Segment Profit (₹ in Million):
  - Plumbing (Plastic): ₹6,126 million
  - Paints and Adhesives: ₹1,150 million
  - Total Segment Profit = ₹6,126 + ₹1,150 = ₹7,276 million

Reconciliation to Consolidated Profit Before Tax (PBT):

- Total Segment Profit: ₹7,276 million
- Add: Unallocated Income/(Expenses) net: ₹162 million
- Less: Finance costs: ₹(413) million
- Less: Share of loss of joint venture: ₹0 million

Consolidated Profit Before Tax (PBT) = ₹7,276 + ₹162 - ₹413 + 0 = ₹7,025 million

From the Directors' Report Financial Highlights (Page 20), the Consolidated Profit Before Tax is stated as ₹6,485 million.

## Summary

The total segment profit of ₹7,276 million reconciles to a Consolidated Profit Before Tax of ₹7,025 million after adding unallocated income/expenses of ₹162 million and deducting finance costs of ₹413 million. However, this reconciled PBT (₹7,025 million) differs from the ₹6,485 million Consolidated PBT stated in the Directors' Report Financial Highlights, indicating that the reconciliation in Note 37 does not explain the ₹6,485 million figure.

## Sources

Astral_Ltd_AR_FY2025_AnnualReport.pdf · Page 208 · FY25  
Astral_Ltd_AR_FY2025_AnnualReport.pdf · Page 20 · FY25

results.question: In Note 37, reconcile total segment profit for 'Plumbing' and 'Paints and Adhesives' to the Consolidated Profit Before Tax of ₹7,025 million, detailing each reconciling item.
results.expected_answer: Note 37 (page 225): total segment profit ₹7,276 million (Plumbing ₹6,126m + Paints and Adhesives ₹1,150m). Reconciliation to PBT ₹7,025m: +₹162m Unallocated Income net, -₹413m Finance costs, ₹0m JV. 7,276+162-413+0=7,025.
results.answer:
## Details

From Note 37 (Segment Reporting) for the year ended March 31, 2025:

- Total Segment Profit:
  - Plumbing: ₹6,126 million
  - Paints and Adhesives: ₹1,150 million
  - Total Segment Profit = ₹6,126 + ₹1,150 = ₹7,276 million

Reconciliation to Consolidated Profit Before Tax (PBT):

- Add: Unallocated Income/(Expenses) (net) = ₹162 million
- Less: Finance costs = ₹413 million
- Less: Share of loss of joint venture = ₹0 million

Calculation of Consolidated PBT:

- Consolidated PBT = Total Segment Profit + Unallocated Income/(Expenses) - Finance costs - Share of loss of joint venture
- Consolidated PBT = ₹7,276 + ₹162 - ₹413 + 0 = ₹7,025 million

## Summary

The total segment profit of ₹7,276 million (Plumbing ₹6,126 million + Paints and Adhesives ₹1,150 million) reconciles to the Consolidated Profit Before Tax of ₹7,025 million by adding unallocated income/expenses of ₹162 million, deducting finance costs of ₹413 million, and accounting for no share of loss from joint ventures.

results.question: What are the key categories of business risk Astral identifies in its 'Managing Risks' section, and what mitigation strategy does the company describe for commodity/raw material price risk?
results.expected_answer: The 'Managing Risks' section (pages ~28-29) lists Supply chain, People, Safety, Foreign currency, Commodity, Competition, and Consumer preference/Obsolescence risks. For commodity risk, mitigation is via strategic inventory positions as a hedge against volatility, purchases aligned to inventory policy, and an explicit avoidance of speculative buying/trading of commodities.
results.answer:
## Details

From the retrieved context, specifically from the Annual Report text sections on risk management ([10] and [11]), Astral identifies several key business risks and describes mitigation strategies for commodity/raw material price risk as follows:

### Key Categories of Business Risk Identified by Astral:
- **Commodity Price Risk:** Risk arising from volatility in prices of raw materials and commodities used in manufacturing.
- **Credit Risk:** Risk that counterparties will default on contractual obligations, leading to financial loss.
- **Currency Volatility Risk:** Impact of foreign exchange fluctuations on procurement and financial planning.
- **Operational Risks:** Including risks related to supply chain, procurement, and inventory management.
- **Energy Risk:** Risk related to energy usage and costs, as mentioned in the responsible business conduct section ([2]).

### Mitigation Strategy for Commodity/Raw Material Price Risk:
- **Widening Sourcing Base:** Expanding the number of suppliers to reduce dependency on any single source.
- **Appropriate Contracts and Commitments:** Entering into contracts that help lock in prices or secure supply.
- **Well-Planned Procurement and Inventory Strategy:** Managing inventory and procurement timing to mitigate price volatility.
- **Risk Management Committee:** The company has a dedicated committee that has developed and enacted a risk mitigation strategy specifically for commodity price risk.

These points are explicitly mentioned in the Annual Report text on pages 153 and 213 ([10], [11]) and summarized in the risk management narrative.

## Summary

Astral identifies commodity price risk, credit risk, currency volatility, operational risks, and energy risk as key business risks. To mitigate commodity/raw material price risk, the company employs a strategy of widening its sourcing base, entering into appropriate contracts, and implementing a well-planned procurement and inventory strategy, overseen by a risk management committee.

results.question: According to the Risk Management section of the report, what are the specific mitigation measures outlined by Astral Ltd to manage 'Supply Chain Disruptions' risk?
results.expected_answer: Per the Risk Management framework table (page 28): diversify supplier base and maintain multiple sources; implement robust supply chain risk management processes; maintain strategic inventory levels of critical raw materials; explore backward integration or local sourcing.
results.answer:
## Details

The retrieved context from Astral Ltd’s FY25 Annual Report includes information on risk management and mitigation strategies related to commodity price risk, credit risk, and supply chain management. Specifically, regarding supply chain disruptions, the following mitigation measures are explicitly mentioned:

- Widening its sourcing base  
- Appropriate contracts and commitments  
- Well planned procurement and inventory strategy  

These points appear in two places (pages 153 and 213 of the Annual Report), indicating a consistent approach to managing risks related to material availability and price volatility, which are key components of supply chain disruption risk.

No other specific or additional mitigation measures for supply chain disruptions are detailed in the retrieved context.

## Summary

Astral Ltd manages the risk of supply chain disruptions through widening its sourcing base, entering into appropriate contracts and commitments, and implementing a well-planned procurement and inventory strategy.

results.question: What targets and timelines does the BRSR set for 'Energy Management' and 'Climate Change and Emissions Management'?
results.expected_answer: Per the BRSR goals/targets section (page 66): Energy Management - 100% electricity from renewable sources by 2040. Climate Change and Emissions Management - Carbon Neutrality (Scope 1,2,3) by 2035, Net-Zero by 2050.
results.answer:
## Details

From the sustainability and BRSR-related disclosures in the FY25 Annual Report (pages 66 and 67):

- **Energy Management Targets:**
  - Achieve ISO 14001 certification for Energy Management by 2026.
  - Attain 100% electricity consumption from renewable sources by 2030.

- **Climate Change and Emissions Management Targets:**
  - Achieve Carbon Neutrality (Scope 1, 2 & 3 emissions) by 2035.
  - Achieve Net-Zero emissions by 2050.

These targets are explicitly stated as part of the company’s sustainability goals and timelines under the BRSR framework.

## Summary

The BRSR sets the following targets and timelines for Astral Limited: ISO 14001 certification for Energy Management by 2026, 100% renewable electricity consumption by 2030, Carbon Neutrality across Scope 1, 2, and 3 emissions by 2035, and Net-Zero emissions by 2050.

## Sources

Astral_Ltd_AR_FY2025_AnnualReport.pdf · Page 66 · FY25

results.question: During the Q2 FY2024 call, management said Astral had increased pipe capacity from 2.90 lakh MT to 3.10 lakh MT after adding 20,000 MT within existing plants. What are the three new plant capacities being added, and what is the total incremental capacity from those three plants?
results.expected_answer: The three new plants are Guwahati 22,000 MT, Hyderabad 70,000 MT, and Kanpur 50,000 MT. The total incremental capacity from these three plants is 1,42,000 MT (22,000 + 70,000 + 50,000).
results.answer:
## Details

From the Q2 FY2024 earnings call transcript:

- Management confirmed that the increase of 20,000 metric tons (MT) capacity was added within existing plants.
- The three plants where capacity additions took place are: Santej, Dholka, and Hosur.
- The total incremental capacity from these three plants combined is 20,000 MT.

This is explicitly stated in the following excerpt:

> Analyst: "Hi! Good evening. Thank you for the opportunity and congratulations for the good set of number on the piping side. First question was on capacity, as I understand you said 20000 tons got added was that Orissa, that is one clarification I needed."

> Management: "No, not Orissa, This Santej plant some addition, some Dholka and some to our Hosur also."

Also, from the broader context:

- The total pipe capacity increased from 290,000 MT to 310,000 MT after this 20,000 MT addition.

## Summary

The three plants where Astral Ltd added pipe capacity during Q2 FY2024 are Santej, Dholka, and Hosur, with a combined incremental capacity addition of 20,000 metric tons.

Management Commentary:
I can add to whatever, Hiranandji said that if you look at our H1 FY2021 to H1 FY2024,
four year CAGR as far as tonnage grows, we are delivering at a 20.5% four year H1 CAGR.
I think we are outperforming in the most of the industries branded players by a long shot
and we have almost doubled our tonnage in four years H1 to H1. So I think we are very
comfortable with that type of tonnage growth we are seeing and we remain bullish with our
guidance to close the year also at a 20% plus tonnage growth level.

Astral Limited
October 19, 2023
margins were healthy and our volume growth was excellent. We have not compromised our
margins in spite of 28% robust volume growth in this quarter and high loaded PVC volume
also. Our gross profit margins were highest in the last four years. We were having EBITDA
margin guidance of 16% to 18%, but we have over surpassed this number and we are
expecting that from the coming quarter the polymer prices are more or less going to bottom
out. We are not seeing much drop from here on little bit may happen because it is very
difficult to predict the number. But it looks that now the seasonal quarters are starting,
seasonal months are starting in India and historically also in the seasonal quarter, the
demand is high, so the prices will not fall. So we are expecting maybe little bit upward
revision of the pricing in the coming time and if that is going to happen that there will be a
conversion of inventory gain in the coming quarters. Of course Q2 will be little pressure
because of the PVC which had dropped in the first 15 days of the month of October.
Looking to the first half of performance of 29% volume growth and lower price polymer,
we are increasing our guidance from 15% to more than 20% volume. That means minimum
20% plus volume growth for the full year. So we will try even more than 20% also. Since
our Gauhati plan has started paint products and shortly we are going to start the pipe also
we are expecting some support from that location also in the second-half of the current year.
We are putting up 22000 metric ton capacity in Gauhati.
During the first half, as Sandeep bhai communicated we have increased our capacity within
our existing plant to the tune of 20000 metric ton. So our capacity has increased from
290,000 metric ton to 310,000 metric tons.
Indian operation of our adhesive business had delivered a very, very healthy not only
volume growth but value growth to the tune of 18%. UK has also delivered both volume
growth and the value growth. Value growth is to the tune of 17%. EBITDA margin were
also very healthy. In Indian operation close about 15.5% and UK was 9.7% little lower than
what normally they deliver 11%, 12%, but we are expecting that this margin to improve
further from UK in the coming quarter because now whatever the high cost inventory there
almost on the verge of completion.
Paint business has shown good recovery compared to the last quarter. You can see that last
quarter was around 40, 41 Crores, this quarter is around 47 Crores and a very healthy
EBITDA of 16%. On Y-on-Y basis we can see there was a drop of 6%. But we are
confident that in the coming quarter we will improve that because now we are settling down
with our new systems and processes and SOPs and the SAP implementation. So I think
mostly our team is getting acquainted and the new Gem Paint is also getting acquainted
with the new system. So we are expecting that it is going to give us a very, very good result
in the coming years.

results.question: In the Q2 FY2024 call, Astral said its water tanks had received a first-ever certification that no other market tank had at that time, and it also claimed a first for its firefighting system. What were those two certifications?
results.expected_answer: The water tanks received NSF certification, and the firefighting system received BSS certification.
results.answer:
## Details

From the Q2 FY2024 earnings call transcript and the Annual Report narrative:

- Astral's water tanks received NSF certification, which was the first time in India for water tanks. This is explicitly mentioned in the Annual Report text (Page 6 and Page 28) and also referenced in the call context as a unique certification for their water tanks.

- Astral’s firefighting system "Fire Pro" received ISI certification for both CPVC pipes and fittings. Astral is the first Indian company to have this ISI certification for both pipes and fittings in the automatic fire sprinkler system category. This is stated in the Annual Report (Page 28).

## Summary

Astral's water tanks received the first-ever NSF certification in India for water tanks, and its firefighting system "Fire Pro" became the first Indian automatic fire sprinkler system to receive ISI certification for both CPVC pipes and fittings.

## Sources

Astral_Ltd_AR_FY2024_AnnualReport.pdf · Pages 6, 28 · FY24

Analyst (Arun Baid) asked:
Welcome to the Q2 FY2024 post results con call. From the management side we have Mr.
Sandeep Engineer – Chairman & Managing Director; Mr. Kairav Engineer – ED; and Mr.
Hiranand Savlani – ED & CFO. Now I hand over the call to Mr. Sandeep Engineer for his
opening remarks, post which we will have the floor open for Q&A. Over to you Sandeepji.

Management answered:
Thank you. Thanks everyone for joining the earning call of Q2 FY2024, and the first half of
FY2024. First of all, I am happy today to share this year Astral is celebrating its 25th
anniversary, which was full of legacy of innovation, connectivity, brand building, wide
distribution, network creation, creation of reach in every corner of India, and building a
strong production capabilities across the country. We started the journey of CPVC pipe
manufacturing and today we can proudly say that we are a building material company
having four different verticals and thousands of SKU's in different verticals. Astral started
its journey in 1998, in the same month of October with single product and single focus to
bring CPVC to India and today we can say with pride that we are the first one to bring
CPVC to India. The first one to make CPVC, a product which replaced the metal piping and
we have created a history of CPVC for 25 years now present in this country and growing
and now multiple manufacturers joining to make CPVC pipes.
As you are well aware, the market situation still continues to be volatile. The polymer
pricing, and the chemical price situation, which is continuously going up and down, but
slowly the PVC prices are now settling down, and CPVC prices of this quarter also come to
a level where it is settled out. But all fluctuations are the challenges of margins and various
growth aspects of the market, but I am very happy that with all these challenges and
volatility, we at Astral are always trying to mitigate these challenges and deliver a
consistent performance on yearly basis.
The result of this is in front of you, once again, we are able to deliver a teamwork and we
have worked hard once again with this volatility also and to give you a volume growth of
25% plus and in a certain quarter in the row. Indian scenario is very robust and seasonally
in the first two quarters which is loaded with PVC product demand with agriculture and
certain grenade pipes. In spite of that our CPVC growth was excellent during downward
pressure trend of polymer we also known the distributors always restock that product and
Page 2 of 27

Analyst (Sanitary and faucets) asked:
I am very happy to announce that our team has achieved the first
milestone of getting 500 stores wherein our display is placed and all these stores have
started selling the product to the end consumer. We are completely cracking the tertiary sale
for me selling to actual users and to the client. The tertiary sale is very important and we are
getting good response from our product line. Product line is growing. We have reached an
average of 6 Crores, but last two months, last month we have been averaging around 8
Crores plus and I am sure that in next two months we will be crossing our breakeven
number of 10 Crores plus and we will continue our growth.
Lots of new product SKU's are getting accepted in the market. We can clearly see that
forthcoming time will be really good for our brand Astral in the vertical as we have worked
very hard to build the brand Astral and the brand Astral itself is a big thing to pull a lot of
our product lines and to make them reach to the consumer with price and with the brand
power.
To summarize, all verticals are doing good. We are going as per our plans and we do not
see any much challenge in the market and we keep giving our consistent growth for our
company as it is known for and as we have always communicated.
Now I hand over to Mr. Hiranand bhai, our ED and CFO for his opening remarks on the
numbers and then myself, Hiranand Bhai and Kairav will answer all your questions and
once again thanks for joining the call and trusting Astral. Thank you very much.

Management answered:
Good afternoon, everyone. Thanks for joining this conference call. I will take you through
the few key numbers of this quarter and then straightway go to the Q&A so that we can
spend more time on the Q&A side.
The revenue numbers are in front of you. This quarter we have grown 16.3% in a
consolidated basis. EBITDA growth was very high compared to the last quarter or even
compared to the last year also and in percentage term it was 17.1% compared to last year
similar quarter was 13.2%.
Page 4 of 27

Astral Limited
October 19, 2023
Now if you break up this consolidated number then I think pipe that is the plumbing which
include the sanitary ware and faucet also which is roughly about Rs.17, Rs.18 Crore. The
value growth was around 17.3% and volume growth was close to about 28% and the
EBITDA growth was around 57% and the percentage term EBITDA was 18%. So we have
guided that we will be working in the range of 16% to 18%. So I am very happy to say that
we are on a higher side of what we have guided and not only that, if we do the adjustment
of inventory losses of CPVC to the tune of 20 Crores and roughly about Rs.4 Crore of loss
of the bath ware, then the adjusted EBITDA will be even much higher.
Now coming to the further breakup of our adhesive and the sealant business. Adhesive
business of India has grown by 18% in value terms and EBITDA was roughly about 15.5%.
Adhesive business in UK grown by 17% and EBITDA was roughly about 10%. Paint
business which was around negative 6% and the EBITDA of 16%.
So these are the key numbers and now I will take you through the few highlights of this
quarter.
This is the second consecutive quarter where we were able to deliver 25% plus volume
growth of our core piping business in spite of having a very high base last year. If you can
see the last year number also you can see that our base was not low in the last year also and
that is why we have given in our press release the last four years CAGR growth also. The
demand scenario is excellent and we are expecting the coming quarter also demand should
remain positive mainly due to good announcement of new projects by a lot of developers,
low polymer prize both CPVC as well as PVC. Lot of infra spending is increasing and we
are seeing a good growth in the housing side also. Across the board when we are seeing the
city wise data, most of the good cities are touching the new high. Most of the cities of India
is having good construction activity and demand is coming from across all the four zones of
country and our new locations are giving very good healthy volume growth than originally
we planned. So we are very excited with the performance of not only our new location in is
the East, but we are very happy with our new product growth such as Sync, Wall, GrainPro,
Silencio and now Bathware also.
As you all are aware that the CPVC prices are on a lower side and we have to also pass the
price reduction to the market and due to that, our realization has dropped compared to
earlier quarters, we have to incur approximately Rs.20 Crores of loss on inventory and our
volume is highest in the turnover, that is why the effect on the inventory is high on our
company. But in spite of that, we were able to deliver a very healthy top line volume and
EBITDA margin.
We have always communicated that Astral is always believing in consistency in
performance and you can see in this quarter also that in spite of inventory loss in CPVC, our
Page 5 of 27

Astral Limited
October 19, 2023
margins were healthy and our volume growth was excellent. We have not compromised our
margins in spite of 28% robust volume growth in this quarter and high loaded PVC volume
also. Our gross profit margins were highest in the last four years. We were having EBITDA
margin guidance of 16% to 18%, but we have over surpassed this number and we are
expecting that from the coming quarter the polymer prices are more or less going to bottom
out. We are not seeing much drop from here on little bit may happen because it is very
difficult to predict the number. But it looks that now the seasonal quarters are starting,
seasonal months are starting in India and historically also in the seasonal quarter, the
demand is high, so the prices will not fall. So we are expecting maybe little bit upward
revision of the pricing in the coming time and if that is going to happen that there will be a
conversion of inventory gain in the coming quarters. Of course Q2 will be little pressure
because of the PVC which had dropped in the first 15 days of the month of October.
Looking to the first half of performance of 29% volume growth and lower price polymer,
we are increasing our guidance from 15% to more than 20% volume. That means minimum
20% plus volume growth for the full year. So we will try even more than 20% also. Since
our Gauhati plan has started paint products and shortly we are going to start the pipe also
we are expecting some support from that location also in the second-half of the current year.
We are putting up 22000 metric ton capacity in Gauhati.
During the first half, as Sandeep bhai communicated we have increased our capacity within
our existing plant to the tune of 20000 metric ton. So our capacity has increased from
290,000 metric ton to 310,000 metric tons.
Indian operation of our adhesive business had delivered a very, very healthy not only
volume growth but value growth to the tune of 18%. UK has also delivered both volume
growth and the value growth. Value growth is to the tune of 17%. EBITDA margin were
also very healthy. In Indian operation close about 15.5% and UK was 9.7% little lower than
what normally they deliver 11%, 12%, but we are expecting that this margin to improve
further from UK in the coming quarter because now whatever the high cost inventory there
almost on the verge of completion.
Paint business has shown good recovery compared to the last quarter. You can see that last
quarter was around 40, 41 Crores, this quarter is around 47 Crores and a very healthy
EBITDA of 16%. On Y-on-Y basis we can see there was a drop of 6%. But we are
confident that in the coming quarter we will improve that because now we are settling down
with our new systems and processes and SOPs and the SAP implementation. So I think
mostly our team is getting acquainted and the new Gem Paint is also getting acquainted
with the new system. So we are expecting that it is going to give us a very, very good result
in the coming years.

results.question: What annual volume growth guidance did Astral management provide for the core plumbing business in their Q1 FY25 conference call?
results.expected_answer: In the Q1 FY25 call, management maintained an annual guidance of 15% volume growth for the full fiscal year, and delivered 16% volume growth in Q1, exceeding the baseline benchmark.
results.answer:
## Details

From the Q1 FY25 earnings call transcript (AstralConcall25Q1.pdf):

- Management indicated that the plumbing business revenue grew from INR 938 crores last year to INR 1,013 crores this year, which is an 8% increase (Page 3).
- Regarding volume growth guidance, the management did not explicitly provide a numeric volume growth guidance in the call.
- When asked about volume growth split between plumbing and infra or urban vs Tier 1/2, management declined to share such confidential details (Page 13).
- On realizations and price assumptions, management indicated mid- to high single-digit growth in realizations if prices remain stable (Page 20).
- No explicit volume growth percentage guidance was given for the plumbing business in the call.

## Summary

Astral management did not provide explicit annual volume growth guidance for the core plumbing business in the Q1 FY25 conference call. They reported an 8% revenue growth year-on-year but declined to share specific volume growth figures or guidance.

## Sources

AstralConcall25Q1.pdf · Pages 3, 13, 20 · FY25

So mid- to high single-digit growth, if the prices remain stable as of now should be assumed,

saying that Q1 was looking low. So from here on, the -- if prices remain same, then we should
