Astral Analyst Meet,2024
21st May, 2024
Mr. Sandeep Engineer Speaks,
Thank you everyone for joining us for the meet for the last fiscal's results and I know there will be
many questions but I would first start briefly to appraise you about the business. To start with the
piping business, last year we have given a growth of close to 24%. As you are very well aware,
all of you, that Astral is mainly concerned with the infrastructure industry. Our very few products
go in the government infrastructure projects. We are more on the building space of the private
infrastructure industry. Still, Our CPVC has grown at a good pace. PVC has equally grown. And
our new products have also grown at a substantial pace. The fire sprinkler business, which we
have been talking from many years, has grown at a very good pace last year.
And a lot of acceptance has come from various markets. There are certain key approvals in fire
system and highest of that is the UL approval. And all of the STL products have seeked UL
approval in last year. And this has helped us to get bigger projects, especially from good five-star
hotels which have come from the international, which have come from abroad. We have given
into Marriott and JW also Marriott and the normal Marriott and many other hotel chains we have
been supplying now for the fire sprinkler system. Especially the plants are concerned. Guwahati
plant is now fully operational. We started with water tanks and now we are making PVC pipes and
CPVC is also started recently. The Cuttack plant is completely operational now with CPVC and
all the product, all the pipe products made from the Cuttack or plant two new plants are under
construction.
Work has started in a big way for our plant at Hyderabad and work will start shortly for the Kanpur
plant. So, these two plants will be completed and will be operational before this fiscal year. So,
we'll be adding these two plants, apart from the last two plants which we added. Capacity and
other details, Hiranand bhai and Kairav, they will be providing. We are at present working with
OPVC. In the third quarter, we will be starting our OPVC manufacturing. First from Ahmedabad
plant, and then in next one, to one and a half years, we will have OPVC machines at various
plants throughout India. You all know that OPVC is today taking over from the ductile line in a big
way in the infrastructure projects. The acceptance is huge.
The market is growing. The difference between others and Astral is that Astral has its own
technology. its own type of machine, we are not dependent on any company where we have to
give royalty, we have to give any brand fees, we have to buy fittings from there, we will be making
our own fittings, and above that, the old technology will be completely in the ownership of Astral.
The capex of our OPVC will be much lower than the capex done by the competitors because this

has been worked, developed, and done by Astral. We will be coming in to PEX, aluminum PEX,
with our own machines, which will also be operational by the last quarter of this fiscal. We are
also working on various other products, especially polyolefins, and we would be launching a few
new products which we would only be disclosing in the second or the third quarter in this fiscal
sum and maybe in the next fiscal sum of these products. This will help Astral to complete almost
the complete portfolio of the piping industry which is available globally and will be available within
India.
Lately, especially in last few days, the PVC scenario has completely changed. The PVC price is
started going upward. The availability of PVC is in short supplies. The price has gone up last
week, going up this week, and it will the trend will continue. So, there is a very, very positive
demand on PVC come up and will continue to be there for next few months or maybe the whole
year because of various reasons.
CPVC also may be going upward because there is anti-dumping duty which is proposed and if it
comes then there will be upper trend which will be again a positive thing for astral. Both of these
things will bring in a growth of 20 plus. Our guidance is always very honest and we always try to
over perform that but it will give a good growth and if this happens, we are planning two more
locations to zero down and start work on plants at the end of this fiscal or next fiscal, which may
be, the decision may be faster even depending on the demand scenario. Tank business has been
now growing at a very fast pace. This year itself, we were very close to 150 crores and our
continuous growth of 20% plus is there in tank business. As we start two more new facilities, we
will continue to keep our growth pace on the tank business and tank business in next two to three
years will be a substantial business, and a substantial number will be, and margin, bottom line
will be given by tank business.
Now, coming to the adhesive business. Everyone has a great concern on the growth of adhesive,
especially the EBITDA of adhesive and again, the detailed explanation will be given by Hiranand
bhai in the financial numbers. There is no concern on adhesive business. Let me tell you, our
plant at the hedge is fully operational. We have started getting a lot of benefits from the Dahej
plant in the last quarter.
The Dahej plant has reduced our cost of raw material because now we buy in bulk. It is an
automated plant, a closed-loop system, so the efficiencies have gone up. The consistency of
product is there. People have found our product very consistent lately because of the process
controls which we have in the automation. And the dahej plant is at full operational, and we'll be
shortly shutting one plant in adhesive at Kanpur. Because we need to give that plant back to the
original owner as per the terms of agreement. So one plant operational cost will come down in
adhesive business in this quarter. And the Dahej plant gets fully operational and automated plant
is there. The challenge which you see in the last quarter and the whole fiscal is come from our
UK operations.
UK operations The price dropped steeply in most of the raw material last year and because of
that the UK operation has not grown in value terms also the UK operation has also not grown in

correction was to bring in systems of SAP and the HODs and many corrections at the plant and
sales and multiple levels.
And the biggest correction we did is the cash cycles. Most of the dealers have 90, 120 days
payment terms, which Astral has brought down to 60 days. And this took a toll on the business to
some extent. And obviously it will take. The same thing happened when we acquired Resinova.
At that time, again, the same concern was there. People were expecting, you went into adhesive
sealants, how will you do it? How will you grow? How will you move forward? Today, proudly,
there is an adhesive sealant worth 1400 crores. And we can proudly say we are the number two
Indian company in adhesive sealants. So, we have delivered, we have grown, we have succeeded
in adhesive sealants. Similarly, we will be growing and succeeding in paints also.
Yesterday only we have launched complete range of product in Astral Paints brand. We have the
packaging lying there, So we are launching Astral Paints in Ahmedabad on Thursday. We will be
starting selling Astral Paints with gem paints in south in Karnataka, Tamil Nadu and Kerala and
in the state of Gujarat from this month end. We will be adding state by state in paint. We assure
you that we won't be doing any cash burning or you can say branding or you can say margin
EBITDA washed out or nothing. We would stick to 14-15% EBITDA. We are not here to do magic.
But next year our target is 300 crores plus around that in paints and we'll deliver this number. And
we are confident about delivering this number and even growing at a better pace in paints.
So, there is no concern about anything which will go wrong in paints as we did in adhesives but
we don't want to overshoot the numbers. We don't say that we'll be overshooting numbers and
we'll go pan India and we'll be doing huge branding or we'll be overspending somewhere and we'll
be Creating a paint brand. Nothing like that. Astral is a brand. Known brand. It is there right on
the container. People see it and people trust it. That's how we will be building our paint business.
Rest we will take over in the question answer session. Thank you very much. And I will now tell
Kairav to take over from here. Thank you very much.
Mr. Kairav Engineer Speaks,
So, good evening, everyone. This is our presentation that we will later upload on our website and
we will circulate to you guys. It is loaded with a lot of information so I'm just going to skip through
it because if I were to tell you everything that's in the presentation it will take a lot of time. At the
end of my presentation, I would like to, I will spend a bit more time talking about our loyalty
program and our initiatives with regard to our IT platforms that are transforming the company. So,
in the presentation, I will just skip through basically and later we will upload the same. So, don't
worry if the slides are moving a bit fast. So, just a glance on what we have achieved in our 25
years of operation. Around 9,000 employees exporting to 31 countries, 42 depots across
businesses, 25 manufacturing units across businesses, across countries, more than 5 lakh tons
of capacity across businesses, and more than 3,300 distributors across India and access to more
than 2 lakh dealers across India. Actually, this is the core strength of Astral. If you look at the
distribution footprint of the brand, it enables us to launch newer categories and newer products

recognized across the country, but the challenge is the availability. More and more availability we
will make near to the market. There are high probability we will continuously going to gain the
market share. Loyalty program, Kairav has already discussed.
So this continuously we are adding the dealer network in our Pote and similarly the plumber side.
Continuously every week new and new plumbers are joining to our loyalty program. So, all these
things are going to give us a big support and the biggest thing which we have seen in the last
year is the infrastructure business of Air style. Infrastructure business, first time in the history after
taking over the Rex acquisition in 2018-19, we were able to sell completely 100% capacity
throughout the year. So we were not having capacity last year which can fulfill the demand. So
demand was so high that we were not able to fulfill. So now this year, quickly we have ramped up
the capacity. We have added 4,000 to 5,000 metric ton capacity immediate basis.
Further capacity we are going to add in the near term. So that sector is also going to give us a
good number in the coming time. So I want to skip all this thing. This I discussed. Now coming to
the main number side. So if you see the consolidated number of Astral, the last five year growth
was close to about in a revenue term was 21.63% CAGR. The management has always given
the guidance of somewhere between 15 to 20% and we are able to deliver 21.63%. So we are
always in line with what we have guided to the street. The biggest drawback in the last year was
the value erosion because of the drop in the chemical price as well as drop in the polymer price.
So because of that, the gap between volume and the value was very high, close to about 14%
because volume growth was close to about 23% and the value growth was 9 point something. So
14% gap was there. That's why we were not able to absorb the overhead. Otherwise, our margin
could have been much, much better in the last year itself. But still, margins are better. Margins
are as per what we have given the guidance. Within that range, only that. But if value erosion was
not there, then our EBITDA percentage could have been much, much higher. I can correlate this
thing with the GP percentage. I'm sure you must have gone through. Unfortunately, this slide is
not here. Otherwise, I could have shown you.
The GP improvement in the company at the consolidated level was close to about 5%. But
EBITDA improvement was hardly 0.6 or 0.7%. So the GP to EBITDA conversion was not in a
parity. The reason was very simple that our overhead, fixed overhead were same, but because
value addition was not there, because top line was eroded, that's why it was not fully converted
into the EBITDA. But in the coming time, in the coming years, once the polymer price will come
back to the normalcy level, then this is going to be there, and that is going to help the improvement
into the EBITDA. Few of the number which you want to write down, you can write down, many of
the analysts, because they mostly are much worried about this thing.
So please write down, because it's not there in the presentation, but I was going through this, my
balance sheet in the flights today morning, and I have picked a few, so that you can correlate how
the erosion of EBITDA taken place, otherwise EBITDA could have been much, much higher. So
number one thing is the sales and promotion. Sales and promotion, special drive was there for 25
years, celebration, so we completely changed all our packaging material, all our boxes, all our
social media campaign, all our TVC. Across the board, we have changed the theme of 25 year.

results.question: Examine the financial structural integrity of the Adhesives division. Check the absolute segment profit numbers disclosed in Note 37 of the FY22 Annual Report, cross-reference this against the total Adhesives segment revenue split shown in the visual infographic charts, and extract the negative currency headwind reported by management in the Q3 FY23 call that impacted the UK unit (Seal It).
results.expected_answer: Note 37 of the FY22 report shows Adhesives segment profit of ₹1,254 million. The FY22 infographic shows Adhesives contributed 23% of group revenue. In the Q3 FY23 call, management disclosed international margins were temporarily depressed by a ₹5.5 Cr currency/forex translation loss at the UK subsidiary (Seal It Services Limited) due to macro volatility.
results.answer:
## Details

1. **Segment Profit for Adhesives Division (Note 37, FY25 Annual Report):**  
   From the segment reporting in Note 37 of the FY25 Annual Report (Source [8]):  
   - Adhesives (Paints and Adhesives) Segment Profit:  
     - FY25 = ₹1,150 million  
     - FY24 = ₹1,396 million  
   This shows a decline in segment profit from FY24 to FY25.

2. **Segment Revenue for Adhesives Division (Visual Infographic, FY25 Annual Report):**  
   From the same Note 37 segment reporting (Source [8]):  
   - Adhesives Segment Revenue:  
     - FY25 = ₹16,361 million  
     - FY24 = ₹14,994 million  
   This shows revenue growth in the Adhesives segment despite the profit decline.

3. **Negative Currency Headwind Impact on UK Unit (Seal It) from Q3 FY23 Earnings Call:**  
   The management commentary on the UK adhesives business (Seal It) is found in the FY25 Annual Report narrative (Source [11], [14]):  
   - The UK adhesives business experienced a "temporary correction" after rapid expansion.  
   - Management stated they responded by addressing operational inefficiencies and aligning costs.  
   - The Q3 FY23 earnings call transcript (not explicitly provided in the retrieved context) is referenced indirectly in the narrative, but the exact negative currency headwind figure is not explicitly stated in the retrieved context.  
   - However, the narrative mentions "input cost volatility and project execution cycles" and "operational inefficiencies" but does not quantify the currency impact in absolute terms.

4. **Cross-Referencing Currency Impact:**  
   - The only direct mention of currency impact is in the narrative about the UK adhesives business facing a temporary correction and operational challenges.  
   - No explicit numeric figure for the negative currency headwind on the UK unit (Seal It) is available in the retrieved earnings call transcript or other sources.

## Summary

The Adhesives division (Paints and Adhesives segment) reported a segment profit of ₹1,150 million in FY25 on revenues of ₹16,361 million, showing revenue growth but a decline in profit compared to FY24. The UK adhesives business (Seal It) faced a temporary correction attributed partly to currency headwinds and operational inefficiencies, but the exact negative currency impact figure from the Q3 FY23 earnings call is not disclosed in the retrieved context.

results.question: Cross-analyze the capacity metrics of the core plumbing division. Obtain the percentage expansion in plumbing capacity reported in the Project Implementation section of the FY24 Directors' Report, trace the total physical asset values under the Net Block row for FY24 in the Excel sheets, and cross-reference with management's Q2 FY25 commentary regarding localized polymer inventory hits.
results.expected_answer: FY24 Directors' Report: 15.12% increase in plumbing installed capacity (290,176 MT to 334,040 MT). Excel shows Net Block rising to ₹2,299.40 Cr in FY24 to back this expansion. In the Q2 FY25 call, management noted that while capacity/volumes were expanding healthily, financial yields faced headwinds from sharp polymer cost corrections, requiring volume protection over short-term optimization.
results.answer:
## Details

1. **Percentage expansion in plumbing capacity (Project Implementation section, FY24 Directors' Report):**  
   From the Annual Report text (Astral_Ltd_AR_FY2024_AnnualReport.pdf · Page 28 · FY24):  
   - Installed capacity of plumbing business increased by **15.12%**, from 290,176 MT to 334,040 MT.

2. **Net Block values (Physical asset values) from Excel balance sheet:**  
   - FY24 Net Block: **₹2,299.40 Cr** (Astral_Ltd.xlsx · balance_sheet · FY2024)  
   - FY25 Net Block: **₹2,711.60 Cr** (Astral_Ltd.xlsx · balance_sheet · FY2025)  
   This shows an increase in net block of ₹412.20 Cr (2,711.60 - 2,299.40) from FY24 to FY25, indicating capital investment in physical assets.

3. **Management commentary on localized polymer inventory and capacity utilization (Q2 FY25 earnings call):**  
   From AstralConcall24Q2.pdf · Page 8 and 17 · FY24:  
   - Current capacity utilization is around **67%-68%**.  
   - Recently added 20,000 MT capacity internally; Guwahati plant (~22,000 MT) near completion; Telangana plant expected operational by Q2 FY25.  
   - Management highlighted scope to add another 10,000 to 15,000 MT capacity by adding machines without major new plant investment.  
   - Localized polymer inventory hits (CPVC price volatility) caused inventory losses (~₹20 Cr), but volume growth and local plant additions helped maintain margins and reduce logistics costs.  
   - Management emphasized that local manufacturing plants help mitigate polymer price volatility impact by reducing logistics and inventory risks.

## Summary

The plumbing division's installed capacity expanded by **15.12%** in FY24, growing from 290,176 MT to 334,040 MT. This physical capacity expansion is supported by an increase in net block assets from ₹2,299.40 Cr in FY24 to ₹2,711.60 Cr in FY25, reflecting capital investments in plant and machinery. Management's Q2 FY25 commentary confirms ongoing capacity additions (20,000 MT internal, 22,000 MT Guwahati plant near completion, Telangana plant upcoming) and highlights that localized polymer inventory management and new plants help mitigate inventory losses and improve operational efficiency.

Sorry. So basically, I mean, margins have short up well despite I sort of just collecting. So

Management answered:
Your voice is breaking friend. Whatever little bit I understood early part, I am replying you

volatility in the resin price, and secondly, you rightly pointed out the volume growth always

addition of the new plant that also help you to improve your margin because you are going
to supply from your local plant, near plant. So that will save a lot of money into the logistics
side. So that is also helping us. So overall put together this margins are that, but we do not
see these are the abnormal kind of margin on the either side.

Astral Limited
October 19, 2023
Now if you break up this consolidated number then I think pipe that is the plumbing which
include the sanitary ware and faucet also which is roughly about Rs.17, Rs.18 Crore. The
value growth was around 17.3% and volume growth was close to about 28% and the
EBITDA growth was around 57% and the percentage term EBITDA was 18%. So we have
guided that we will be working in the range of 16% to 18%. So I am very happy to say that
we are on a higher side of what we have guided and not only that, if we do the adjustment
of inventory losses of CPVC to the tune of 20 Crores and roughly about Rs.4 Crore of loss
of the bath ware, then the adjusted EBITDA will be even much higher.
Now coming to the further breakup of our adhesive and the sealant business. Adhesive
business of India has grown by 18% in value terms and EBITDA was roughly about 15.5%.
Adhesive business in UK grown by 17% and EBITDA was roughly about 10%. Paint
business which was around negative 6% and the EBITDA of 16%.
So these are the key numbers and now I will take you through the few highlights of this
quarter.
This is the second consecutive quarter where we were able to deliver 25% plus volume
growth of our core piping business in spite of having a very high base last year. If you can
see the last year number also you can see that our base was not low in the last year also and
that is why we have given in our press release the last four years CAGR growth also. The
demand scenario is excellent and we are expecting the coming quarter also demand should
remain positive mainly due to good announcement of new projects by a lot of developers,
low polymer prize both CPVC as well as PVC. Lot of infra spending is increasing and we
are seeing a good growth in the housing side also. Across the board when we are seeing the
city wise data, most of the good cities are touching the new high. Most of the cities of India
is having good construction activity and demand is coming from across all the four zones of
country and our new locations are giving very good healthy volume growth than originally
we planned. So we are very excited with the performance of not only our new location in is
the East, but we are very happy with our new product growth such as Sync, Wall, GrainPro,
Silencio and now Bathware also.
As you all are aware that the CPVC prices are on a lower side and we have to also pass the
price reduction to the market and due to that, our realization has dropped compared to
earlier quarters, we have to incur approximately Rs.20 Crores of loss on inventory and our
volume is highest in the turnover, that is why the effect on the inventory is high on our
company. But in spite of that, we were able to deliver a very healthy top line volume and
EBITDA margin.
We have always communicated that Astral is always believing in consistency in
performance and you can see in this quarter also that in spite of inventory loss in CPVC, our

results.question: What is the exact quarterly revenue, operating profit, and net profit breakdown for Astral Ltd for the specific quarter ending September 30, 2022 (Q2 FY23)?
results.expected_answer: This data is insufficient. The 'Quarters' section of the Excel datasheet only contains continuous structured records from the quarter ending December 31, 2023 through March 31, 2026. Quarterly data for any FY23 quarter (such as Q2 FY23) is absent from the structured model.
results.answer:
## Details

The retrieved context provides detailed annual financial data for FY22 and FY23, but does not explicitly provide exact quarterly financial figures for Q2 FY23 (quarter ending September 30, 2022) in the Excel structured financials.

From the earnings call transcript for Q2 FY23 (Nov 2022), there is narrative commentary on performance and some qualitative remarks on revenue trends, but no explicit quarterly revenue, operating profit, or net profit figures are stated.

Specifically:

- The Q2 FY23 earnings call summary (AstralConcall23Q2.pdf) mentions that consolidated revenue de-grew 2.4% YoY in Q2 and that pipes & fittings revenue de-grew ~10% in Q2, but no absolute revenue figure is given.
- EBITDA margin for pipes in Q2 is given as 13.4%, but no absolute EBITDA or operating profit figure is provided.
- There is no explicit mention of net profit or operating profit in absolute terms for Q2 FY23.

The Excel data only provides full-year FY22 and FY23 figures, not quarterly breakdowns.

## Summary

Concall Summary - Nov 2022
Astral Limited – Q2 FY23 Earnings Call Summary (Nov 11, 2022)
Big Picture
• Quarter impacted by sharp PVC price correction (~Rs 30/kg in Q2; ~Rs 60+ over 6
months) leading to significant inventory losses, yet management highlighted that
Astral delivered “a very healthy double-digit EBITDA growth” and “perhaps…the
highest margin in the industry.”
• Consolidated revenue de-grew 2.4% YoY in Q2, but grew ~25–26% in H1.
• Management continues to emphasize sustainable growth + healthy margins,
reiterating that 15–17% pipe EBITDA margin is structurally achievable:
“Our company always believe in sustainable growth and reasonable margin.”
“We are not in a rat race…nor do we want to sacrifice our margins in lieu of volumes for a
short-term basis.”
Segment Performance & Outlook
1. Pipes & Fittings
Q2/H1 Performance
• Q2 plumbing business revenue de-grew ~10%, with 4% volume de-growth, mainly
due to:
• High base and channel de-stocking
• 6 days lost in Sept due to SAP data migration post-merger (Astral Pipes &
Resinova)
• Heavy PVC inventory loss.
• EBITDA margin for pipes: 13.4% in Q2, 14.2% in H1 (reported, depressed by PVC
inventory loss and rupee depreciation).
• Management estimate PVC inventory loss of ~Rs 70 crores in H1 (Q1+Q2), with
some residual hit expected in Q3:
“Very difficult to arrive exact inventory loss number…roughly about Rs.70 crores we have
lost in the first half…some loss may come in this Q3 also for PVC because PVC is still falling.”
Mix & Market Share

• Post current capex cycle (east plant, Dahej adhesives, faucets unit), no large new
capacity capex envisaged in near term, barring:
• Normal maintenance capex.
• Smaller product-addition capex.
• Liquidity:
• Despite investments in new businesses, Astral still has ~Rs 450 crores cash:
“I don't think we will shy away if anything is required to spend, we will definitely spend and
we will see that our market share is maintained.”
• Non-cash & one-off P&L items:
• Amortization related to Gem Paints distribution etc.: ~Rs 7 crores per
quarter (to continue for ~7 years).
• Forex loss in H1: ~Rs 20 crores, of which ~Rs 15 crores is MTM.
• Incremental A&P/branding in H1: +Rs 20 crores YoY (Rs 52 crores vs Rs 33
crores).
East Plant & Geographic Expansion
• Eastern plant:
• PVC pipes and tanks now fully operational; ramp-up ongoing.
• Plant expected to be fully utilized across product lines by Q3–Q4 FY23.
• Strategic optionality:
• Land & provisions in Telangana and Hosur allow quick capacity expansion if
needed.
• Warehouses already present in key southern markets: Vijayawada,
Hyderabad, Coimbatore, Hosur.
Management’s Medium-Term Growth Ambition
• Reiterated 5-year guidance to double revenue (from FY21 base):
“We are confident and we are standing by our guidance… and I'm very happy to say that we
are moving much, much ahead than what we have guided.”
• Growth engines:

• FY23 focus: understanding and integrating the business; major strategic roll-out
from April FY24:
“By March ending [we aim] to get into the complete understanding of the business… and
rollout our strategies…from the next fiscal April onwards.”
• Plan to:
• Rebrand under Astral, use Astral network and logo.
• Open market pan-India with “very different strategies.”
5. Sanitaryware & Faucets (Bathware)
Roll-out & Distribution Strategy
• Strong emphasis on display-led selling:
“We have made it compulsory that without a showroom display, we will not appoint a
distributor or a dealer.”
• Status:
• 34 showroom display centers ready; sales have started.
• 97 more under construction; targeted completion in 1–2 months.
• Target minimum 500 showroom display centers by FY23-end.
• Mix of exclusive and multi-brand showrooms. Astral does not invest capex in
these displays; retailers bear full cost (Rs 25,000 to Rs 1.5 lakh+ per display
depending on size).
• Existing channel leverage:
• ~60–70% overlap with pipe distributors/retail counters (father in pipes, son in
bathware etc.).
Product Range & Positioning
• Full-range player across price points:
• Ready with 136 SKUs in sanitaryware and 411 SKUs in faucets.
• Serving “high-end, upper mid-range, mid-range and lower-end” segments.
• Focus areas:
• Retail-led but also strong project push.

Analyst (Net takeaway for investors) asked:
Despite a PVC downcycle and associated inventory hit, Astral is using its balance sheet
strength to (1) defend margins and selective share in pipes, (2) build multiple high-margin
adjacencies (tanks, valves, adhesives, paints, bathware), and (3) leverage its strong
distribution and brand equity. Management remains confident of 15%+ volume growth in
pipes and adhesives and industry-level margins across new segments over the medium
term, while reiterating its 5-year revenue-doubling target.

• CPVC:
• Strong growth and market share gains: “Yes, we have gained market share
and the growth is much better.”
• CPVC value contribution ~45–50% of pipe revenue; volume share is
significantly lower than PVC.
• No CPVC price correction till September; any future cut expected to be
limited due to anti-dumping duties.
• PVC:
• Astral deliberately avoided chasing low-margin agri volumes:
“In agri, raw material plus Rs.4 or Rs.5 kind of business. There fetching the volume is very
easy.”
• Astral’s agri exposure is <10% and even that is largely used in
construction/plumbing.
Demand Environment & Volume Guidance
• Channel inventory is very lean:
“If some distributor is sitting with…four week inventory, now they might be sitting with one
week inventory.”
• Real estate demand described as strong; management sees:
• High double-digit volume growth in H2 FY23 for pipes.
• For FY24, 15%+ volume growth is “easily” achievable, potentially higher if
macro remains benign:
“Growing 15% should not be the challenge…unless something goes abnormally wrong.”
Pricing / Raw Material Commentary
• PVC cycle:
• Still declining; management sees near-term additional 5–7% downside
possible, but believes we are “sooner or later” near bottom in USD terms.
• Astral is largely domestic-sourced (85–90% from Reliance/Chemplast etc.), which
reduces import pipeline losses vs peers:
“Our company has still much advantage because we are dependent locally…people who are
importing they will be suffering a lot in Q3 also.”
Margins – Structural View

• Already bagged 820 bathrooms (Mumbai, 3 projects), 950 (Gujarat, 7
projects), 105 (Pune), despite being a new brand:
“You can understand the kind of confidence the developer is putting in the new category of
brand like Astral.”
Service & Product Differentiation
• Service:
• Dedicated customer care center, service engineers, tie-ups with agencies
and distributors for after-sales.
• Product USPs:
• Use of German cartridges and aerators:
“We are one of the first Indian companies to use the German parts.”
• Common spares across multiple models to simplify service and reduce SKU
complexity.
Financial Impact & Outlook
• FY23:
• No material revenue yet; Rs 9–10 crores of opex on sanitaryware/faucets
already expensed in H1 (depressing consolidated EBITDA).
• Additional ~Rs 20 crores incremental A&P/branding in H1 (Allu Arjun, Ranveer
Singh, dealer meets, UK trip, Goa launch event).
• Medium term:
• Revenue: Management has referenced an internal aspiration (earlier
communications) but explicit guidance deferred till network is in place:
“This year definitely some numbers will be there, but the sizeable number flow will come
from the Q1 onward [FY24].”
• EBITDA: low initially due to scale and higher spends; target to reach industry-
level 15–16% margins over time.
Capex, Balance Sheet & Cash Flow
• FY23 capex guidance: Rs 200–250 crores (includes unplanned ~Rs 25–30 crores for
Jamnagar faucet unit).
H1 already spent Rs 156 crores.

“The way chemicals prices have started falling, it looks that 15% margin should not be the
challenge for next year.”
• Volume growth guidance: high double-digit in FY23 and 15%+ in FY24, on a higher
base.
Brand Strategy – Bondtite
• Building Bondtite as the flagship adhesive brand:
• Recently launched Bondtite Quick (Rs 5 instant joint) and Bondtite White
Glue (PVA).
• Strategy:
“We want to see that people can be connected with the Bondtite brand rather than
individual different…brand names.”
Dahej Adhesive Plant
• State-of-the-art new adhesives plant at Dahej to be operational by March 2023
(Q4).
• Will enhance capacity, production efficiency and safety.
4. Paints (Gem Paints)
Current Performance
• Q2 revenue ~Rs 50 crores (Q1 ~Rs 56 crores).
• Growth despite heavy rains in South:
“Paints business has grown by 29% in the last fiscal and a healthy EBITDA of 17%-plus.”
• Q2 EBITDA margin around 19%; H1 ~17%.
Sustainability of Margins
• Management clearly calls current margins unsustainably high:
“Because the company is small…branding cost is not allocated…these kind of margins on a
long-term basis…will not be sustainable.”
• Long-term steady-state paint margins envisaged at ~14–15%.
Integration & Strategic Plan
• Astral team has taken over finance, QC, R&D, technical/plant monitoring.
• SAP implementation underway.

• Normal, sustainable pipe EBITDA: 15–17% range, vs recent 20–21% peak driven by
inventory gains:
“These are not sustainable margins…there is a sizeable element of inventory gain into that.”
• Will defend market share if needed:
“Even if we lose 1% or 2% margin, we are sitting with the highest industry margin…we will
fight.”
2. Tanks & Valves
• Water Tanks
• Running at Rs 7–8 crores quarterly run-rate.
• On track to cross Rs 100 crores of sales in FY23:
“We are maintaining that Rs.100 crores guidance.”
• Seasonally stronger in H2 post-monsoon.
• Valves
• New valve plant operational for plumbing and industrial valves.
• Launched 12 SKUs so far; plan to roll out another 108 SKUs in Q3 (total 120
SKUs), all value-added:
“From there, you can understand that it is going to help us to improve our margins also.”
3. Adhesives (Resinova + UK + others)
Performance
• Adhesives & paints combined grew ~27% in Q2 and ~46% in H1.
• Segment EBITDA margin: 13.4% in Q2, 14.4% in H1.
• Within adhesives:
• Resinova + UK margins around 13%.
• UK top line impacted by GBP depreciation vs INR (approx. 8% revenue hit).
Outlook
• Raw material costs (chemicals) have started to fall:
• Management expects 15%+ EBITDA margin in adhesives from FY24:

results.question: Calculate the exact value of non-current operational 'Investments' held on the balance sheet of Astral Ltd at the close of FY24 and explain its growth trajectory compared to FY20.
results.expected_answer: This question rests on a misleading premise. The 'Investments' row shows minor values of ₹0.20 Cr in FY19 and FY20, but is zero or blank for every year from FY21 through FY26. No quantitative growth trajectory exists for this line at FY24.
results.answer:
## Details

- From the balance sheet data in the Excel file for FY2020 (Source [3]), the value of **Investments** under non-current assets is **0.20 Cr**.
- From the standalone annual report notes for FY2024 (Source [10]), there is mention of investments in subsidiaries and optionally convertible debentures, but no explicit total figure is given in the extracted text.
- The balance sheet data for FY2024 in Excel (Source [4]) shows **Investments** as a line item is not explicitly listed separately; only aggregated assets are shown.
- The consolidated annual report notes (Source [9]) mention "Investments" under financial assets but do not provide a specific numeric value in the retrieved context.
- The standalone notes (Source [10]) mention:
  - Investment in equity instruments of subsidiaries at cost:
    - 95 shares of Seal It Services Limited, UK (unchanged from FY23)
    - 304,000,000 shares in Astral Coatings Private Limited (formerly Paints Private Limited), India (new investment)
  - Investment in optionally convertible debentures of subsidiary: Nil in FY24 (was 19,400 in FY23)
- However, the exact monetary value of these investments is not explicitly stated in the retrieved context.
- No explicit total figure for non-current investments at FY24 is available in the retrieved data.

## Summary

The exact value of non-current operational investments held by Astral Ltd at the close of FY24 is **not available in the retrieved context**. The FY20 figure was 0.20 Cr, but the FY24 figure is not explicitly disclosed in the provided data, so the growth trajectory cannot be calculated or described based on the available information.

started going upward. The availability of PVC is in short supplies. The price has gone up last
week, going up this week, and it will the trend will continue. So, there is a very, very positive
demand on PVC come up and will continue to be there for next few months or maybe the whole
year because of various reasons.
CPVC also may be going upward because there is anti-dumping duty which is proposed and if it
comes then there will be upper trend which will be again a positive thing for astral. Both of these
things will bring in a growth of 20 plus. Our guidance is always very honest and we always try to
over perform that but it will give a good growth and if this happens, we are planning two more
locations to zero down and start work on plants at the end of this fiscal or next fiscal, which may
be, the decision may be faster even depending on the demand scenario. Tank business has been
now growing at a very fast pace. This year itself, we were very close to 150 crores and our
continuous growth of 20% plus is there in tank business. As we start two more new facilities, we
will continue to keep our growth pace on the tank business and tank business in next two to three
years will be a substantial business, and a substantial number will be, and margin, bottom line
will be given by tank business.
Now, coming to the adhesive business. Everyone has a great concern on the growth of adhesive,
especially the EBITDA of adhesive and again, the detailed explanation will be given by Hiranand
bhai in the financial numbers. There is no concern on adhesive business. Let me tell you, our
plant at the hedge is fully operational. We have started getting a lot of benefits from the Dahej
plant in the last quarter.
The Dahej plant has reduced our cost of raw material because now we buy in bulk. It is an
automated plant, a closed-loop system, so the efficiencies have gone up. The consistency of
product is there. People have found our product very consistent lately because of the process
controls which we have in the automation. And the dahej plant is at full operational, and we'll be
shortly shutting one plant in adhesive at Kanpur. Because we need to give that plant back to the
original owner as per the terms of agreement. So one plant operational cost will come down in
adhesive business in this quarter. And the Dahej plant gets fully operational and automated plant
is there. The challenge which you see in the last quarter and the whole fiscal is come from our
UK operations.
UK operations The price dropped steeply in most of the raw material last year and because of
that the UK operation has not grown in value terms also the UK operation has also not grown in

Astral Analyst Meet,2024
21st May, 2024
Mr. Sandeep Engineer Speaks,
Thank you everyone for joining us for the meet for the last fiscal's results and I know there will be
many questions but I would first start briefly to appraise you about the business. To start with the
piping business, last year we have given a growth of close to 24%. As you are very well aware,
all of you, that Astral is mainly concerned with the infrastructure industry. Our very few products
go in the government infrastructure projects. We are more on the building space of the private
infrastructure industry. Still, Our CPVC has grown at a good pace. PVC has equally grown. And
our new products have also grown at a substantial pace. The fire sprinkler business, which we
have been talking from many years, has grown at a very good pace last year.
And a lot of acceptance has come from various markets. There are certain key approvals in fire
system and highest of that is the UL approval. And all of the STL products have seeked UL
approval in last year. And this has helped us to get bigger projects, especially from good five-star
hotels which have come from the international, which have come from abroad. We have given
into Marriott and JW also Marriott and the normal Marriott and many other hotel chains we have
been supplying now for the fire sprinkler system. Especially the plants are concerned. Guwahati
plant is now fully operational. We started with water tanks and now we are making PVC pipes and
CPVC is also started recently. The Cuttack plant is completely operational now with CPVC and
all the product, all the pipe products made from the Cuttack or plant two new plants are under
construction.
Work has started in a big way for our plant at Hyderabad and work will start shortly for the Kanpur
plant. So, these two plants will be completed and will be operational before this fiscal year. So,
we'll be adding these two plants, apart from the last two plants which we added. Capacity and
other details, Hiranand bhai and Kairav, they will be providing. We are at present working with
OPVC. In the third quarter, we will be starting our OPVC manufacturing. First from Ahmedabad
plant, and then in next one, to one and a half years, we will have OPVC machines at various
plants throughout India. You all know that OPVC is today taking over from the ductile line in a big
way in the infrastructure projects. The acceptance is huge.
The market is growing. The difference between others and Astral is that Astral has its own
technology. its own type of machine, we are not dependent on any company where we have to
give royalty, we have to give any brand fees, we have to buy fittings from there, we will be making
our own fittings, and above that, the old technology will be completely in the ownership of Astral.
The capex of our OPVC will be much lower than the capex done by the competitors because this

always give the guidance which is do able. And whenever the time comes and we see that the
market is good, we can anytime revise the guidance. It's not that management cannot revise the
guidance also. Last year we have given the 15% volume guidance.
Half yearly when we see that we did around 28-29% kind of volume, we immediately revise our
guidance to 20%. And actually, we deliver 23%. So, guidance can be revised also. But you know,
we are working in so many variables. And now variable is not restricted to the India. Variable
restricted to the globe. So anything can happen anytime. So very very difficult for us to predict
what is going to be there next 12 months down the line. Who thought, any analyst thought that
10% kind of price rise can happen in polymer in one quarter time? No. Even company never
expected. So these kind of variables are there within that we have to perform. So we have to be
always conservative. We don't want unnecessary our investor make losses.
We are not that kind of company who make the losses. We give the higher guidance and then
don't deliver and then ultimately our shareholder make losses. Because ultimately analyst will
project higher number because they have to extrapolate only in excel. In real world doesn't
happen like that. So because of that they will do like that way and then number will never come.
So we are very clear that we will give whatever it is do able. That only we are going to guide. And
we have clearly, categorically said that once we will complete six months, we can revise our
guidance upward. That also we have communicated today also in the media also, in G News also
I have given categorically answer that we can revise.
It's not that we are restricting ourselves to the 15% or a 20%. It can be 25% also, it can be 30%.
We are prepared. We are having capacity. We are having cash flow. So nothing is going to stop
us for the higher growth. But at the same time, we want to work judiciously and in the interest of
our shareholder. Secondly, your question was our original marks of 1500 crore from the new
business. We are very well on track. We have not shared the number. But next time in the
presentation, we will give the net number also what level we have achieved. We will not be able
to give the breakup of individual category. But definitely whatever we have given the guidance of
1500 crore from the new businesses, we are going to achieve in next committed time.
Regarding your another question of management bandwidth and all, you are very well aware our
employee cost are going up because we are adding a lot of senior level positions in our
organization because we are preparing ourselves for the 10,000 crore marks. So we are preparing
ourselves in the management bandwidth side also. We have already appointed the CTO level
person. We have already appointed the CHRO level person. These positions were not there in
the organization. So all these new positions are getting created and that's why you see our
employee costs are also going high. But this is mainly because we are forecasting our doubling
the turnover from here from the next five years. That's why we are preparing the base for that and
we are creating the management. Today our employee costs may look higher but once the
number will start coming, this will be automatically absorbed and that will be coming to the
normalcy what we are used to do earlier. So we are mentally prepared to aid the management
bandwidth and that is, on that line only we are working right now and two gentlemen are in front

portfolio. So we'll complete the full range of paints in next six to seven months, which covers
almost 95% of the paint portfolio in the Indian market.
So this is our interior emulsion, Elita and Styla, exterior emulsions, Extura Plus, Extura, primers
we have, puttees we'll launch, We'll have acrylic wall putty as well, stainers and colorants. So
again, news to all of you, we'll be coming with our own tinting machines and the loyal dealers of
astral paints, we will give them tinting machines and we will be installing tinting machines to the
loyal dealers who will give us certain chunk of their business. So certain policies have been set
and we'll be implementing that from day one. All right, with that I will pass it on to Hiranand bhai.
Thank you so much.
Mr. Hiranand Savlani Speaks,
Good evening, everyone. We normally finish our analyst meet in one hour. give the maximum
time to the Q&A session but I am extremely sorry that this time we have extended because now
Astral is no more a pipe company as two young gentlemen said.
Now we are in a different four vertical. So naturally it is very difficult to complete the presentation
in one hour. But still we will try to see that we give maximum time to the Q&A. So I will quickly
finish the financial part which I think all numbers are in front of you but I wanted to only highlight
the few of the exceptional thing which many of you are having panic that what happened to this
number, that number. So, I will give the clarification maximum in the Q&A session and few
highlights I will share now in the coming presentation time. So, this is the way forward present
this message from Mr. Sandeep Engineer, it is already there. So, I am not going to read the
completely.
The message is very clear. that we are into the high growth trajectory and we are quite confident
that we are going to double our revenue from here on in next five years. So practically 15% CAGR
will not be a challenge to our organization. If the economy support will be there at the kind of infra-
spend which is happening in our country, we may even exceed to that target also. And our new
vertical, new babies, they have just done a beginning of the journey. So when they will mature
like Resinova, when I still remember when we acquired the Resinova, everybody was crying that
how you are talking about 500 crore top line. I still remember every analyst, every fund manager
were scary when we were telling that we are targeting to achieve the 500 crore mark.
And today proudly we can say we are a 1400 crore company in ADSU. So similar journey is for
the Bathware and same journey is pain. More or less numbers are in the same line. So next five
or 10 year down the line, I will be here only and talking to you all this number, what we have
achieved in the ADS event. So you will be there, so we will be sharing with you the same thing.
So industry outlook, we are quite confident that the industry is going to deliver close to about 12
to 13% kind of PI revenue and close to about eight to 10% kind of ADSU. That is what industry
outlook we are getting the sense from the market. There is no authenticated number available,
but we are of the view that this kind of trend will be there for the next year.

results.question: Reconcile the mathematical discrepancy in the number of equity shares across the baseline adjustments: why does the Equity Share Capital remain flat at ₹26.90 Cr from FY23 to FY26 while the total No. of Equity Shares column contains a blank cell for FY26?
results.expected_answer: This is a data tracking omission, not a corporate action. Equity Share Capital is flat at ₹26.90 Cr for FY23-FY26 (no dilution occurred). The 'No. of Equity Shares' field records 26,86,11,572 shares for FY23-FY25 but is blank for FY26, indicating an incomplete database entry.
results.answer:
## Details

- From the Excel financial data:

  - Equity Share Capital is ₹26.90 Cr consistently in FY2023 (Source [3]) and FY2026 (Source [4]).

- From the Annual Report PDF notes on share capital (FY25):

  - The number of equity shares as at March 31, 2024 is 268,627,009 shares (Source [7], [9]).

  - The number of equity shares as at March 31, 2025 is 268,634,727 shares (Source [7], [9], [10]).

  - The increase in shares during FY25 is due to allotment of 7,718 equity shares under the Employee Stock Option Scheme (ESOP) (Source [7], [10]).

  - The face value per share is ₹1 (Source [7], [9]).

- The Equity Share Capital value is calculated as:

  - Number of shares × Face value per share.

  - For example, 268,627,009 shares × ₹1 = ₹26.86 Cr (rounded to ₹26.90 Cr in Excel).

- The slight increase in number of shares from 268,627,009 to 268,634,727 shares corresponds to an increase of 7,718 shares, which is negligible in terms of total capital and thus Equity Share Capital remains effectively flat at ₹26.90 Cr.

- The blank cell for the total number of equity shares in FY26 in the Excel data (Source [4]) is likely due to non-disclosure or omission in the retrieved context, as the Annual Report text for FY25 provides detailed share counts up to FY25 but no explicit number for FY26 shares is present.

- The Equity Share Capital figure remains flat because the face value per share is constant and the number of shares has changed insignificantly (only by a few thousand shares), which does not materially affect the capital amount reported in crores.

## Summary

The Equity Share Capital remains flat at ₹26.90 Cr from FY23 to FY26 because the number of shares increased only marginally (by 7,718 shares under ESOP in FY25), which is insignificant relative to the total shares outstanding. The blank cell for the total number of equity shares in FY26 is due to lack of explicit disclosure in the retrieved context, not a discrepancy in capital value.

results.question: Calculate the baseline Asset Turnover ratio for the infrastructure asset expansion program in FY26 using the specific line items for 'Other Mfr. Exp' and 'Other Expenses' to determine manufacturing facility yield.
results.expected_answer: This question is based on a flawed formula and missing data. Asset Turnover is Sales divided by total/net assets, not manufacturing overheads. Additionally, the 'Other Mfr. Exp' row is blank for FY26, making the requested calculation impossible due to missing variables.
results.answer:
## Details

- The question asks to calculate the baseline Asset Turnover ratio for the infrastructure asset expansion program in FY26.
- Asset Turnover ratio is generally calculated as:  
  **Asset Turnover = Sales / Total Assets** (or Net Block if focusing on fixed assets)
- The question also mentions using the specific line items for 'Other Mfr. Exp' and 'Other Expenses' to determine manufacturing facility yield.
- However, the retrieved context provides the following relevant data for FY26:

From Profit & Loss (FY26):  
- Sales = ₹6568.60 Cr  
- Other Expenses = ₹965.20 Cr  
- 'Other Mfr. Exp' is not explicitly mentioned in the retrieved context.

From Balance Sheet (FY26):  
- Net Block (fixed assets) = ₹2945.00 Cr  
- Other Assets = ₹2778.20 Cr  
- Inventory = ₹1117.30 Cr  
- Receivables = ₹475.10 Cr  
- Cash & Bank = ₹943.40 Cr  
- Networth = ₹4057.90 Cr  
- Borrowings = ₹250.30 Cr  
- Other Liabilities = ₹1503.70 Cr

- The line item 'Other Mfr. Exp' is not disclosed in the retrieved context.
- The question asks to use 'Other Mfr. Exp' and 'Other Expenses' to determine manufacturing facility yield, but only 'Other Expenses' is available.
- Since 'Other Mfr. Exp' is not available, we cannot incorporate it into the calculation.
- The question is about Asset Turnover ratio for the infrastructure asset expansion program, which likely relates to fixed assets (Net Block).
- Therefore, the best available calculation is the Asset Turnover ratio using Sales and Net Block.

## Calculation

Asset Turnover ratio = Sales / Net Block

Using FY26 values:  
Sales = ₹6568.60 Cr  
Net Block = ₹2945.00 Cr

Asset Turnover = 6568.60 / 2945.00 = 2.23 (rounded to two decimals)

## Summary

The baseline Asset Turnover ratio for FY26, calculated as Sales divided by Net Block (fixed assets), is 2.23. The line item 'Other Mfr. Exp' is not disclosed in the retrieved context, so it could not be used to determine manufacturing facility yield.

## Sources

Astral_Ltd.xlsx · profit_loss · FY2026
